companyDescription:
Cidestra is a Management Consulting firm focusing on Strategy and Transformation within the Financial Services sector. Cidestra is part of the Newground Alliance - an alliance of independent specialist consulting firms.

We provide deep insights into the most critical disruptive forces within Financial Services: Emerging Technologies, Regulatory Pressure and Changing Customer Behaviour. By combining these insights with industry expertise and strong skills within innovation, design and transformation, we help our clients to be in the forefront of the market development.

We have advised and supported the leading financial institutions and Fintechs in the Nordics and in Europe in a wide range of initiatives, ranging from Strategy to Design and Implementation.
companySlogan: Cidestra is a Management Consulting firm focusing on Strategy and Transformation within the Financial Services Sector.
descriptionHtml:
Cidestra is seeking a dynamic management consultant within Financial Crime Prevention to join our team. In this role, you will assist our clients within Banking, Insurance and Fintech to reshape their operations within Financial Crime Prevention.

This is an exciting opportunity for a proactive, results- and development driven professional who thrives in a fast-paced, client-focused, dynamic learning environment and wants to work within the first line of defense. You will play a crucial role in shaping and implementing solutions for client challenges, particularly within Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Financial Crime Prevention.

Key Responsibilities

  • Collaborate with clients to understand their business needs and challenges
  • Design and implementing Financial Crime prevention frameworks and components – incl. KYC, screening, risk assessments, risk models, monitoring, reporting
  • Build strong client relationships and ensure exceptional service delivery
  • Communicate solutions & change needs and manage key stakeholders
  • Lead change streams and initiatives and coordinate with cross-functional departments

Key Requirements

  • 4-13 years of experience of change initiatives within Financial Crime Prevention – in management consulting OR within Banking and Finance sector coupled with management consulting experience
  • Proven background in business analysis, project management and/or management of agile development coupled with good analytical skills and a problem-solving mindset
  • Experience in business design and transformation
  • Subject-matter knowledge and experience from the financial services industry, especially within AML / Financial Crime Prevention
  • Experience working within the first line of defense
  • Passion for innovation and disruption
  • Good communication skills - fluency in Swedish and English

What We Can Offer

To be part of an ambitious, forward-thinking company with a flat hierarchal structure giving you direct access to the partners and senior colleagues

To work in an environment based upon trust, where we collaborate and everyone is seen as a valued member of a tight team. We believe that coaching, learning, collaboration, well-being, having fun and feeling psychological safety is the foundation for a high performing team!

To be engaged on some of the most interesting and complex projects in the Financial Services sector, especially in the areas of payments, open finance, financial crime prevention and sustainability.

Personal development and career progression based upon your own individual merit and initiatives, which is not ruled by a hierarchal culture or process.

Cidestra offers a competitive compensation model creating great opportunity for successful candidates.

To be part of Newground Alliance, an alliance of twelve companies, that employ 260 consultants giving you the opportunity of being involved in a larger ecosystem of cultures and opportunities.

#Cidestra

Cidestra is a management consulting firm focusing on strategy and transformation within the Financial Services industry.

We have advised the leading financial institutions in the Nordics and in Europe in a wide range of initiatives, ranging from Strategy to Design and Implementation.

Our company is part of the Newground Alliance - an alliance of independent niche consulting firms with various areas of expertise. The group has in total over 250 employees with office at Norrmalmstorg 14 in Stockholm.

companyDescription: Modern banking. Real-time control. Juni provides business banking, spend management, accounting and financing, on one powerful platform and app.
companySlogan: Modern business banking. Real-time control.
descriptionHtml:
Juni who? 👋

At Juni, we’re busy building the future of banking. We want to help businesses do more with less and get access to the financial tools they need to operate, grow, and win globally.

How & Why We Do It 🚀

We give our people the same thing we’re building for our customers: freedom. Freedom to be 100% yourself. Freedom to explore your potential and what’s more – make the most of it. We truly believe we’re making the world a better place for online businesses, and we want you to be a part of that mission.

Your role at a glance 🤸‍♀️

Join our dynamic team at Juni as a Compliance Officer or Senior Compliance officer. You will play a crucial role in ensuring our operations adhere to all relevant regulatory requirements, supporting our mission to provide secure and compliant financial services. This role offers the opportunity to work in a fast-paced environment, contributing to both strategic and operational compliance initiatives.

Your Responsibilities 👏

In this role, you’ll:

  • Implement and oversee monitoring programs to ensure adherence to regulatory requirements and industry standards
  • Conduct compliance risk assessments and monitoring controls
  • Provide guidance and support on compliance-related queries
  • Participate and conduct New Product Approval Process (NPAP) assessments
  • Design and deliver training programs to enhance compliance awareness among employees
  • Manage Juni’s data privacy related controls from a second line perspective
  • Monitor and participate in incident handling
  • Draft and review Juni’s policies, ensuring alignment with current regulations
  • Prepare and submit compliance and regulatory reports to authorities, senior management and the Board as required
  • Secretary and point of contact to Juni’s Governance, Risk & Compliance Committee
  • Act as the internal liaison between internal audit and the rest of the business

Tooling ⚙️

  • Slack
  • G-suite
  • Linear
  • Miro
  • Steep
  • Sana LMS

Qualifications 👑

What we need to see

  • Fluent in Swedish and English
  • Must reside in Sweden
  • Law degree
  • Min. 2 years of experience in a second line of defense role within the financial sector
  • Min. 2 years of experience in an AML/CTF first line of defense role within the financial sector
  • Strong understanding of regulatory requirements and industry standard
  • Practical experience with payment related regulations

What we’d love to see

  • Previous experience as a Data Protection Officer (DPO)
  • Prior experience in fraud prevention, either in the first or second line of defense
  • Previous experience in sanctions domain

Your people 👥

Our team is as ambitious as our amazing customers. We aim high and we move with speed to make our vision a reality. We care deeply about building a better future for our customers and each other. Here, you can work with people at the top of their game and who didn’t get there by playing games. You can help us create a whole new category in financial services.

Your Benefits 😍

We’re freedom-first. Transparent. Caring. Empowering. So our benefits are too.

  • We work hybrid. We'll see you in one of our offices in Stockholm or Gothenburg at least two days per week.
  • Swap 2D for 3D. Meet all Junis IRL at the company onsite each year.
  • Diversity is at our core. We're part Swedish. Part Canadian. Part French. Part Indian. Part Italian. Part British. Part Portuguese. You get the idea.
  • Great players can stay great players. Progress your career whether you choose to manage people or not.
  • Stock options. We can’t promise you’ll make a fortune. But we’ll give it our very best shot.
  • Vacation days. 30 days. Awesome.
  • Private Health insurance. You know. Just in case.
  • Beautiful offices in central Gothenburg and Stockholm, front row sea view!

At Juni, we’re building the future of banking. We want to help businesses do more with less and get access to the financial tools they need to operate, grow, and win globally.

descriptionHtml:
Cybersphere Limited is a leader in the field of data protection, offering innovative solutions based on advanced cryptographic methods and blockchain technology. Since 1999, we have been building corporate infrastructure with a focus on security, high automation, and real-world business cases. Our systems work in real time, are resistant to cyber attacks, and are ready to grow with you.

The Role

You Will Be Responsible For

  • Conducting penetration testing, simulating an attack on the system to find exploitable weaknesses.
  • Developing and implementing security framework, policies, processes/procedures and guidelines.
  • Maintaining security subject-matter expertise and keep abreast of best practices & trends.
  • Managing vulnerability assessment.
  • Oversight & resolution of security incidents.

Ideal Profile

  • You possess a Degree/Diploma in Computer Science, Engineering or related field.
  • You have at least 2 years experience, ideally within a IT Security role.
  • You have good interpersonal and communication skills and are adept at working with multiple stakeholders to drive desired outcomes.
  • You have working knowledge of Multi-sig, KYC/AML and ICO / Token Sales
  • You are a strong networker & relationship builder
  • You possess strong analytical skills and are comfortable dealing with numerical data
  • You are adaptable and thrive in changing environments

What's on Offer?

  • Work within a company with a solid track record of success
  • Work alongside & learn from best in class talent
  • Leadership Role

companyDescription:
SJR in Sweden (SJR) är ett av Sveriges ledande och mest erfarna bolag inom rekrytering och konsultlösningar. Vår grundläggande idé är att hjälpa företag med rätt kompetens och människor med rätt utmaning. Vi skapar möjligheter för människor att utvecklas, och därmed organisationer att växa hållbart. SJR har blivit utnämnt som ett Karriärföretag 2024. 

Ända sedan starten 1993 har vi varit specialiserade inom såväl personlighetsbedömning som de områden vi rekryterar till, vilket ger oss en unik förmåga att utifrån högt ställda krav matcha rätt kompetens med rätt uppdragsgivare. Vi erbjuder specialistkompetens inom ekonomi och finans, HR och lön, Supply Chain och inköp, CSRD- och hålllbarhetsroller, IT och hybrid och chefsroller.

SJR är idag cirka 350 medarbetare och konsulter. Vi jobbar med kunder, konsulter och kandidater över hela Sverige – med kontor i Stockholm, Uppsala, Göteborg, Malmö och Helsingborg.
companySlogan: Ett av Sveriges ledande och mest erfarna konsult- och rekryteringsbolag. Vi skapar trygghet för er!
descriptionHtml:
Vill du bidra i arbetet med att motverka finansiell brottslighet och har erfarenhet inom transaktionsmonitorering? Då är du den vi söker!


Om tjänsten

Vår kund är ett av Nordens ledande finansbolag som på den svenska marknaden har ett brett utbud av finanseringstjänster gentemot både företag och privatpersoner genom leasing och avbetalning. Kunden sitter centralt i Stockholms City och du kommer ingå i ett AML team om 18 personer. Önskade start är omgående och 8 månader framåt med goda chanser till förlängning.


Ansvarsområden

I din roll som AML- analytiker kommer du vara en viktig del i arbetet med utredningar kopplade till KYC och AML. Du kommer främst att arbeta med transaktionsmonitorering och rapportering till myndigheter. Arbetet innefattar med att utreda transaktioner som avviker från de normala för vidare åtgärd. Du kommer att rapportera misstänkta transaktioner vidare internt som en del av kundens arbete med att bekämpa ekonomisk brottslighet.


Lämplig bakgrund

Vi söker dig som har erfarenhet av transaktionsmonitorering/övervakning inom AML/KYC-området. Vi lägger stor vikt vid personliga egenskaper och söker dig som är positiv, serviceinriktad och ansvarsfull samtidigt som du som har förmågan att skapa förtroende, arbeta strukturerat och leverera en god service. Du trivs i ett högt tempo och är en lagspelare inriktad på det gemensamma resultatet. Vidare är du bra på att kommunicera både i tal och skrift.


Ansökan

För mer information om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta Atena Kamaliha. Vi intervjuar löpande och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan ansökningstiden har gått ut. Sista ansökningsdag är 2025-10-02.

Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!

companyDescription:
Sparbanken Eken är en nytänkande bank med en stark strävan att skapa värde för våra kunder och regionen där vi verkar. Vi erbjuder allt en storbank kan erbjuda, men med lokalt fokus. Vi har ca 70 medarbetare och kontor i Ryd, Delary, Grimslöv, Åryd, Älmeboda och Långasjö. Huvudkontoret finns i Ryd. 

Att vi är lokalt ägda, har små enheter/kontor och gillar att ge tillbaka till närsamhället skapar stolthet, både internt och hos våra kunder.
companySlogan: Världen är din, hos oss.
descriptionHtml:
Bli en del av Sparbanken Eken som AML-utredare!

Är du passionerad över att skydda våra finansiella system mot penningtvätt och finansiering av terrorism? Då har vi den perfekta möjligheten för dig inom Sparbanken Ekens säkerhetsavdelning. I denna tjänst kommer du att spela en nyckelroll i att skydda samhället från finansiell brottslighet och engagera dig i varierande och spännande uppgifter inom säkerhet och IT. Du får möjlighet att förbättra dina färdigheter inom ett område som är kritiskt både för banken och samhället.


Vem söker vi?

Vi söker dig med intresse av penningtvättsbekämpning (AML) och motverkande av terrorismfinansiering. Meriterande är erfarenhet av AML-arbete samt god kännedom om regelverk och branschtrender. Vi ser gärna att du har en examen inom juridik eller ekonomi, alternativt motsvarande erfarenhet.

Vi ser mycket positivt på om du har intresse och erfarenhet inom systemutveckling och digitalisering då dessa färdigheter blir allt viktigare för effektiv utredning och övrig IT-utveckling inom banken.


Om tjänsten

I denna position kommer du att utreda misstänkta aktiviteter relaterade till AML från olika källor, samt övervaka transaktioner och dokumentera varningar enligt fastställda protokoll. Du ansvarar för noggranna kundutredningar inklusive hantering av korrespondens, och fungerar som rådgivare till kollegor i frågor gällande AML. Snabba svar på myndighetsförfrågningar med noggrannhet är avgörande, liksom att förbereda rapporter till myndigheter såsom Finanspolisen.

För att lyckas hos oss behöver du hålla dig informerad genom utbildningar om utvecklande regelverk och metoder, samt driva förbättringar inom våra AML-processer för ökad effektivitet.

Vi ser att personlig lämplighet är avgörande därför genomförs bakgrundskontroller vid anställning och periodiskt därefter enligt policykrav.


Vad vi erbjuder dig

Hos oss är varje medarbetare viktig. Vi tror att du växer som mest när du får förtroende, möjlighet att påverka och ett tydligt syfte i ditt arbete. På Sparbanken Eken får du allt detta – i en framtidsinriktad fullservicebank med stark lokal närvaro och höga ambitioner. Här är du med och driver utvecklingen för både våra kunder och för den region vi verkar i.


Därför erbjuder vi dig:

  • Ett meningsfullt uppdrag – du är med och förverkligar idéer och drömmar för människor och företag i vår region.
  • En hållbar arbetsmiljö – ett generöst friskvårdsbidrag, regelbundna hälsokontroller och massage på arbetstid.
  • Trygghet och förmåner – du erbjuds sjukvårdsförsäkring, förmånliga villkor på bankens produkter och tjänster samt vår resultatbaserade bonus "Guldeken".
  • Möjlighet att påverka – vi är en platt organisation där idéer välkomnas och beslut tas nära verksamheten.
  • Ett samhällsengagemang att vara stolt över – sedan starten 2008 har vi delat ut över 50 miljoner kronor till lokala projekt inom utbildning, kultur och föreningsliv.


Ansökan

  • Tjänsten är tillsvidare och på 100 %.
  • Tillträde enligt överenskommelse.
  • Placeringsort efter individuell överenskommelse på något av våra bankkontor.
  • Ansökningar hanteras av vår HR-ansvarig Cecilia Turnstedt, hr@sparbankeneken.se, telefon 072-235 41 29
  • Frågor om tjänsten besvaras av Mats Clarin, Säkerhetschef telefon 073 – 827 28 03.
  • Vid önskad kontakt med facklig kontaktperson hör du av dig till HR-ansvarig Cecilia Turnstedt som hänvisar dig vidare till rätt person.


Sista ansökningsdag är den 7 september. Vi kommer dock att genomföra urval och intervjuer löpande, så tveka inte att skicka in din ansökan så snart som möjligt.


Ansök genom att skicka in CV och personligt brev till: hr@sparbankeneken.se


Läs mer om hur det är att jobba hos oss.

Välkommen med din ansökan!
descriptionText: Bli en del av Sparbanken Eken som AML-utredare!Är du passionerad över att skydda våra finansiella system mot penningtvätt och finansiering av terrorism? companyDescription: Aduna is a landmark venture between some of the world's leading telecom operators and Ericsson, dedicated to enabling developers worldwide to accelerate innovation by leveraging networks to their full potential via common network Application Programming Interfaces (APIs). Its venture partners include AT&T, Bharti Airtel, Deutsche Telekom, e&, KDDI, Orange, Reliance Jio, Singtel, Telefonica, Telstra, T-Mobile, Verizon and Vodafone. Aduna’s developer partner platforms include Google Cloud, Infobip, Sinch, and Vonage. By combining network APIs from multiple operators globally under a unified platform based on the CAMARA open-source project, driven by the GSMA and the Linux Foundation, Aduna provides a standardized platform to foster collaboration, enhance user experiences, and drive industry growth.
companySlogan: Innovation powered by network APIs
descriptionHtml:
All Together, Extraordinary

www.adunaglobal.com

We bring together the best minds in communications technology to unify the telecom industry driving simplicity, scalability and efficiency. This is more than a job—it’s a chance to revolutionize the future of global connectivity. Together, we make the extraordinary happen.

Founded by world leaders in communications technology, Aduna unites and opens mobile networks worldwide to the wider developer community, fueling innovation which gives rise to a new programable connectivity ecosystem. We are a start-up of scale. This will be never business-as-usual. We run in an agile environment, iterating, and improving not just what we do, but how we do it. We foster a culture of trust and simplicity with a global perspective

The breakthroughs we make here impact people, businesses, and societies worldwide.


This role can be based in Sweden, Poland or on in the EST time zone for Canada.


Ready to create what’s next?


About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Product Manager to help define the future of network APIs, shaping products that enable developers and enterprises to seamlessly integrate advanced network capabilities into their applications. This is a true 0→1 opportunity to build and launch platform APIs in an evolving space. You’ll drive product vision, strategy, and execution in close collaboration with engineering, GTM, and ecosystem stakeholders. The role requires a mix of technical acumen, product leadership, and ecosystem fluency with the ability to navigate complexity and influence industry standards (e.g., CAMARA). You will be accountable for the success of your product area end-to-end — from design and integration through commercialization and evolution.


What You’ll Be Doing

Product Strategy & Execution

· Define and evolve the API product roadmap based on market needs, technical possibilities, customer feedback, and ecosystem signals

· Drive prioritization decisions across a portfolio of APIs and evolving product areas

· Shape and communicate the product value proposition and strategy — ensuring alignment with business goals and customer outcomes

API Platform Leadership

· Collaborate with engineering to define clear product and API requirements, integration expectations, and experience goals

· Take ownership of key API design choices including versioning, auth flows, and observability to deliver reliable and consistent developer experience

· Identify and remove friction points in the developer journey — from onboarding to ongoing integration

Standards & Ecosystem Influence

· Represent the company in industry forums such as CAMARA and GSMA Open Gateway, TMF

· Actively influence the direction of standards based on customer needs, developer feedback, and internal product efficiency

· Translate evolving standards into concrete product plans and prioritization change

Competitive & Market Positioning

· Conduct competitive analysis across APIs, features, and industry players

· Identify areas of differentiation and craft a strategy to win in a fast-evolving ecosystem

· Continuously monitor trends to ensure the product remains relevant, competitive, and innovative

Commercialization & Service Design

· Define and maintain service schedules, pricing models, SLAs, and usage terms for commercial APIs

· Act as SPOC and product SME across sales, legal, operations, and customer engagements

· Launch products with the right packaging and positioning to meet customer needs and support business growth

Lifecycle & Outcome Ownership

· Evaluate product lifecycle stage and make data-driven decisions on where to invest, sunset, or iterate

· Track key adoption, usage, and performance metrics — and take corrective action as needed

· Regularly revisit the product’s market fit to ensure it remains efficient, scalable, and competitive


What We’re Looking For

· 8-10 years of experience in product management, ideally with API platforms, infrastructure, or developer tools

· Strong technical fluency — you can engage on architecture, authentication, systems design, and API integration

· Proven track record of end-to-end product ownership — from strategy to delivery and iteration

· Excellent stakeholder skills — capable of aligning engineering, sales, legal, and standards bodies

· Experience working with or contributing to industry standards (e.g., CAMARA, GSMA) is a strong plus

· Analytical and structured — you make data-informed decisions and drive clarity in ambiguity

Bonus Points

· Knowledge of telecom, network slicing, exposure, differentiated connectivity, auth and fraud, KYC

· Familiarity with API monetization, developer portals, SDKs, or API management tools

· Experience debugging complex, layered issues

· Strategic, commercial, and systems thinking

· Clear, confident written and verbal communication

· Comfortable presenting to executives and external stakeholders

· High ownership and bias for action — moves fast with discipline

· Passion for simplifying complexity and scaling operational excellence

· Strong collaboration across technical, legal, and customer-facing teams

companyDescription: Ikano Bank creates possibilities for better living by offering simple, fair, and affordable services, enabling a healthy economy for the many people and businesses. Its offer includes savings and loan products for consumers, sales support services for retailers, and leasing and factoring solutions for businesses. Ikano Bank operates in Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Norway, the UK, Poland, Germany and Austria, and is a part of Ingka Group. Ikano Bank’s head office is located in Malmö, Sweden and the company is registered in Älmhult, Sweden where the business was once founded..
companySlogan: Together, we create a great place to work where people are inspired to be the best they can be.
descriptionHtml:
About the role

As the B2B Fraud Excellence Specialist at Ikano Bank, you will play a crucial role in protecting the bank and its customers from fraud. You will be the organization’s subject matter expert in B2B fraud, with a key focus on the Swedish market.


Your daily work will involve both preventive actions and fraud investigations, ranging from simpler cases to more complex, high-risk matters. You will drive and implement preventive measures, ensuring consistent practices across the Nordic markets, and take ownership of regular reporting and analysis to monitor fraud trends and effectiveness of controls. You will collaborate closely with internal stakeholders in Commercial, Credit, AML & KYC as well as Collection to deliver a comprehensive fraud risk strategy with a commercial mindset. Maintaining a proactive and curious mindset, you will identify new tools and innovative methods to enhance the bank’s fraud prevention and detection capabilities, improving efficiency and effectiveness.


In This Role, Your Key Responsibilities Will Include

  • Managing daily fraud prevention activities and fraud investigations, ranging from minor to complex cases, with a focus on the Swedish market.
  • Developing and implementing new and effective controls across our Nordic markets to continuously strengthen the mitigation of fraud risks in B2B Leasing and Factoring.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally and across markets to ensure a holistic approach to fraud issues.
  • Providing training and guidance to colleagues and external partners on fraud-related matters.
  • Participate in and represent Fraud in various internal and external forums.
  • Keeping up-to-date with emerging fraud trends and threats, adapting strategies as needed to ensure the fraud risk remains within the bank’s risk appetite.

The role is based in Sundbyberg, Stockholm.


The last application date is September 14th.


About You

We are looking for a candidate with strong analytical skills and proven experience in one or more of the following areas: fraud prevention, late collections, or credit risk. Previous experience within the B2B leasing and/or factoring industry is considered a strong advantage.


You have an academic background in Business Administration, Criminology, or Law — or possess at least five years of equivalent and relevant professional experience.


You Bring

  • Proven experience in leading initiatives across markets.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
  • Fluency in Swedish and professional proficiency in English.


We Also See You As Someone Who

  • Is self-driven, proactive, and takes ownership.
  • Thrives in dynamic environments and manages multiple priorities effectively.
  • Has strong customer focus and understands the business context of fraud and/or credit risks.
  • Demonstrates excellent problem-solving and investigative skills.
  • Communicates clearly and confidently, both verbally and in writing.
  • Works collaboratively across functions and organizational boundaries.


We will place great emphasis on personal suitability, as well as your ability to work both independently and collaboratively across teams.

About Ikano Bank

Ikano Bank creates possibilities for better living by offering simple, fair, and affordable services, enabling a healthy economy for the many people and businesses. Its offer includes savings and loan products for consumers, sales support services for retailers, and leasing and factoring solutions for businesses. Ikano Bank operates in Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Norway, the UK, Poland, Germany and Austria, and is a part of Ingka Group. Ikano Bank’s head office is located in Malmö, Sweden and the company is registered in Älmhult, Sweden where the business was once founded.


Contact information

In this recruitment, Ikano Bank collaborates with Adecco.

If you have any questions about the role or the recruitment process, you are welcome to contact the responsible recruiter:

Marie Ekvall at marie.ekvall@adecco.se


We look forward to receiving your application!

descriptionHtml:
Job description:

Group Financial Crime Prevention (GFCP) consists of different units: Fraud Management, Sanctions, KYC, Transaction Monitoring and more. Several of these units need automated customer communication through different channels such as SMS, Push or Chat. In Fraud Management we have our strategy for customer communication during customer journey and high level requirements in place. In a common meeting at GFCP level it was decided that we need to find out, understand and document high level customer communication requirements from other GFCP units so that a future solution will be capable of handling other GFCP units’ communication needs than only Fraud Management. We want to build a GFCP level capability for automated communication with our customers.

Scope Requirements from Sanctions, Know Your Customer (KYC), Transaction Monitoring (TM) and Fraud (FM) will be gathered and summarized. Representatives from these units need to be interviewed 1 by 1 and/or in big workshops or through other information gathering structures. Key persons from Fraud Management shall be invited to these sessions and support the consultant. The client believe that recurring alignment and reporting meeting with FM dedicated people responsible for this work is needed continuously.

Preferred consultant location is Stockholm, Sweden.

The outcome of this piece of work shall be

  • A slide pack describing the work done, explaining different GFCP units needs and how the final requirement scope has been concluded. Quality to the standard that it can be used for senior manager presentation.
  • Business requirements (Functional + non-functional) preferably expressed by Use Cases (UML graphs + necessary text) so the future design work will be easier and steps can be taken towards choosing a final solution for automated customer communication.

Duration: ASAP - 20250930

Language: English & Swedish

Required skills:

Experience of working with Banks/Financial Services

Preferred skills:

Fraud Management

companyDescription:
Born from the acquisition of LeasePlan by ALD Automotive, part of the Societe Generale group, Ayvens is a leading global sustainable mobility player committed to making life and business flow better.

By bringing together complementary capabilities and expertise built over decades, our legacy companies saw an opportunity to reimagine mobility as we know it, providing full-service leasing, flexible subscription services, fleet management and multi-mobility solutions to large international corporates, SMEs, professionals and private individuals. 

With more than 14,500 employees across 42 countries, 3.4 million vehicles and the world’s largest multi-brand EV fleet, we’re leveraging our unique position to lead the way to net zero and spearhead the digital transformation of the mobility sector.

Ayvens. Better with every move.
descriptionHtml:
At Ayvens, progress starts with you.

Our ambitions to shape the future of sustainable mobility are powered by our talent. Join us, and get better with every move.

Compliance Officer (Vikariat under föräldraledighet)

Om Rollen

Vill du vara med och hjälpa Ayvens Sweden att bygga ett toppmodernt compliance-ramverk och leda dess implementering? Om svaret är ja, då söker vi dig. Som lokal Compliance Officer kommer du att ansvara för både implementering och övervakning av compliance-programmen, som syftar till att minska risker såsom beteenderisker, juridiska och etiska risker. Rollen är avgörande för att säkerställa att Ayvens Sweden följer alla relevanta lagar, förordningar och interna policys. Du rapporterar till Risk- och Compliance Director och blir en del av ett dedikerat team om fem personer med nära kontakt med organisationen samt daglig kontakt med Ayvens Group/Société Générale.

Dina Arbetsuppgifter Inkluderar Bland Annat

  • Utveckla och underhålla lokala compliance-ramverk i linje med lagkrav, samt säkerställa att interna rutiner följer både lokala regelverk och krav från Ayvens Group/Société Générale.
  • Underhålla KYX-krav för organisationen och övervaka deras genomförande.
  • Säkerställa att Ayvens Sweden uppfyller krav på bekämpning av penningtvätt och finansiering av terrorism, både ur ett lokalt och Ayvens Group/Société Générale-perspektiv.
  • Genomföra compliance-riskbedömningar i hela organisationen, utveckla strategier och handlingsplaner för att mildra identifierade risker samt följa upp genomförandet.
  • Undersöka och rapportera compliance-överträdelser eller brott.
  • Ta fram regelbundna compliance-rapporter till ledningsgruppen.
  • Organisera och genomföra compliance-utbildningar (policyefterlevnad, intressekonflikter, ABC, AML/CTF etc.) för medarbetare.
  • Bevaka juridiska och regulatoriska förändringar och säkerställa att dessa kommuniceras i tid till organisationen.

Kompetenskrav

  • Kandidatexamen inom juridik, ekonomi eller annan relevant utbildning, med minst fem års erfarenhet inom compliance-området.
  • God kunskap om compliance-reglering i Sverige samt praktisk erfarenhet av implementering och underhåll av compliance-ramverk.
  • Flytande tal och skrift i både svenska och engelska.
  • Erfarenhet från leasing, finanssektorn eller större konsultföretag är meriterande.

Vem är du?

Vi värderar högt din förmåga att navigera i komplexa situationer, ditt självständiga arbetssätt samt din förmåga att samarbeta effektivt både inom och utanför organisationen. Eftersom du kommer att ha en rådgivande och relationsskapande funktion är stark kommunikativ förmåga viktig, liksom förmågan att anpassa budskap efter mottagare. Är du framåtgående, positiv och en verkställare passar du perfekt in i denna roll.

Tjänsten är ett heltidsvikariat mellan perioden 2025-11-01 – 2026-09-30 på Ayvens Sweden AB med kontor i Solna. Möjlighet till hybridarbete i samråd med närmsta chef. Urval och intervjuer sker löpande.

Why Ayvens?

With over 3.4 million vehicles managed across more than 42 countries, we provide full-service leasing, flexible subscription services, fleet management services and multi-mobility solutions to customers of all sizes, including large corporates, SMEs, professionals, and private individuals. By leveraging our unique position to lead the way to net zero and further shape the digital transformation of the industry, we are well-positioned to meet the evolving mobility needs of our clients and provide them with the solutions they need to thrive.

At Ayvens, we believe that our success is driven by our commitment to customer satisfaction. Our team is dedicated to delivering innovative solutions and technology-enabled services that help our customers focus on their everyday business. We’re committed to sustainable mobility and have made it a core part of our strategy. In everything we do, we’re guided by the principles of authenticity, curiosity, commitment and collaboration. We aim to foster an organisation that's diverse in people and ideas, where everyone can thrive and be themselves, no matter who they are.

Join us on this exciting journey as we continue to enable the transformation towards large scale adoption of sustainable mobility and provide our customers with the solutions they need to succeed. Follow our page for the latest updates, news, and insights.

  • ALD Automotive | LeasePlan are rebranding to Ayvens across all 42 countries by 2025.

companyDescription:
The Web Manuals document management system supports aviation professionals with the Editing, Distribution and Compliance Monitoring of their manuals with agility and control. 

We work with a global perspective building partnerships with the world's leading experts and suppliers in aviation compliance management. 

Web Manuals Store includes complementary products and services such as templates manuals and compliance linking. 

We support over 620 operations in all segments of aviation. Book a demo to find out how we can help you take control of your documentation. We'd love to chat with you!
companySlogan: The All-in-One Document Management System For Aviation Professionals
descriptionHtml:
Hey there! We are Web Manuals, a fast-growing SaaS company working towards digitizing the aviation industry. Both our customers and our product is constantly in the clouds but for us the sky is not the limit! We want to continue to grow until we reach the stars but as a bootstrapped company, we do it on our own terms. We are a young, fun, and diverse team that loves to explore new places together. Apply today to be part of our global team!

Web Manuals is truly a place where you can grow, both professionally and personally, and where you can explore the world and try your wings in an innovative environment. We offer an international setting with offices in Malmö (HQ), San Diego, New York, Sydney and Singapore. We keep on growing - and are now looking for a Product Compliance Associate to join our team in Malmö!

About The Role

As a Product Compliance Associate, you will play a vital role in our journey towards digitizing aviation. Your main task will be to quality assure and transfer regulations, standards, and documentation into Web Manuals.

Core responsibilities includes, but are not limited to:

  • To monitor direct sources of the Compliance Libraries and ensure that new content is fed into our database.
  • Update and maintain Compliance Libraries with data from Store partners.
  • To keep internally controlled Libraries up to date.
  • Ensuring the accuracy and quality of our Compliance Libraries content.
  • To assist in feeding improvements into the backlog regarding compliance features and product updates.
  • To assist in improving the Compliance Import Process as needed.

To thrive in this role, we believe that you have:

  • A university degree or equivalent education.
  • A structured approach to work.
  • Excellent collaboration and teamwork skills.
  • Keen attention to detail.
  • Self-motivation and a growth mindset.
  • Strong organizational skills.
  • Clear and professional communication skills in English, both in speech and in writing as English is our corporate language

Ideally, you also have:

  • Proficiency in Word, Excel, and similar tools.
  • Experience from a similar role.

As a person, we believe that you are:

  • Quality-minded and well-organized.
  • Eager to learn and develop.
  • Enthusiastic about working in a dynamic team environment.

Additional information:

Start date: ASAP

Work extent: Full-time

Available positions: 1

Location: Malmö

Other information: We utilize Predictive Index (PI) assessment tool in all our recruitment processes. By leveraging PI, we aim to ensure a strong alignment between the candidate's competencies and our company culture, while minimizing potential biases in decision-making

companyDescription:
ROYC is a leading B2B financial technology company that provides a complete private markets operating system, empowering private equity firms, banks, wealth managers, and multi-family offices to seamlessly access, distribute, and manage private investments at scale.
  
As private markets expand, financial institutions require scalable, technology-driven solutions to manage complexity, optimizing fund operations, and delivering exceptional client experiences.

ROYC combines state-of-the art private markets technology with tailored fund structuring and investment services. Our intuitive, scalable platform replaces manual processes with automation and real-time data access, transforming how private market investments are managed across the entire fund lifecycle.
companySlogan: Investment Operating System. Private Markets Investments at Scale.
descriptionHtml:
About ROYC

ROYC is the leading European B2B financial technology company that provides a complete private markets operating system, empowering private equity firms, banks, wealth managers, and multi-family offices to seamlessly access, distribute, and manage private investments at scale. As private markets expand, financial institutions require scalable, technology-driven solutions to manage complexity, optimizing fund operations, and delivering exceptional client experiences. ROYC combines state-of-the-art private markets technology with tailored fund structuring and investment solutions. Its intuitive, scalable platform replaces manual processes with automation and real-time data access, transforming how private market investments are managed across the entire fund lifecycle.

Role Purpose

As an Investor Service Officer at ROYC, you will serve as the primary point of contact for our sophisticated investor base. You’ll ensure a seamless experience from onboarding through ongoing support—managing accounts, coordinating transactions, liaising with external administrators, and partnering closely with both Sales and Technology teams to continuously enhance our platform.

Role Description

  • Investor Onboarding & Support
    • Guide new investors through account setup, KYC/AML checks, and documentation
    • Respond promptly to inquiries on transactions, statements, and platform functionality
  • Third-Party Relationship Management
    • Own day-to-day interactions with fund administrators, depositories, and distribution partners
    • Monitor service levels, escalate issues, and drive continuous improvement
  • Cross-Functional Partnership
    • Collaborate with Sales to support investor pitches and proposal follow-up
    • Work alongside Tech to surface user feedback, prioritize enhancements, and test new features
  • Process Optimization & Compliance
    • Embed best practices in processing procedures, controls, and reporting
    • Identify gaps, propose solutions, and implement process improvements across the investment organization
What We’re Looking For

  • Experience
  • 2–5 years in private equity, venture capital, or fund administration—servicing HNWIs or institutional clients
  • Domain Knowledge
  • Solid grasp of investor due diligence requirements, fund structures, and private-market products
  • Technical Savvy
  • Proficiency with CRM tools, investor platforms, and Excel modelling
  • Communication & Service Excellence
  • Exceptional verbal and written skills in English (French fluency a plus)
  • Demonstrated track record of delivering high-touch, client-centric experiences
  • Personal Attributes
  • Rigorous attention to detail and strong organizational skills
  • Problem-solver with tenacity to thrive in a fast-paced environment
  • Collaborative mindset and intellectual curiosity to help build a market-leading platform

Key Competencies

  • Problem Solving: Analyze and resolve investor issues quickly and effectively
  • Teamwork: Build strong partnerships across Sales, Operations, Compliance, and Technology
  • Time Management: Prioritize tasks and meet deadlines without sacrificing quality
  • Client Feedback: Actively solicit, interpret, and act on investor insights to enhance service levels
  • Accountability: Take initiative and ownership over your work and relationships

Preferred Profile

  • Recent experience of private equity or venture capital either for an investment firm or fund administrator.
  • Strong understanding of investor due diligence requirements, investment products, and fund structures within the private markets.
  • Good organizational and administration skills with high attention to detail.
  • Strong interpersonal skills.
  • Strong oral and written communication skills. Preferably English and French
  • Excellent client service skills
  • Driven and intellectually curious, having an ambition to play a key role in building a market leading solution in the private markets.

companyDescription: Ikano Bank creates possibilities for better living by offering simple, fair, and affordable services, enabling a healthy economy for the many people and businesses. Its offer includes savings and loan products for consumers, sales support services for retailers, and leasing and factoring solutions for businesses. Ikano Bank operates in Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Norway, the UK, Poland, Germany and Austria, and is a part of Ingka Group. Ikano Bank’s head office is located in Malmö, Sweden and the company is registered in Älmhult, Sweden where the business was once founded..
companySlogan: Together, we create a great place to work where people are inspired to be the best they can be.
descriptionHtml:
As our Compliance Officer, you will be a member of a fantastic global team of 10 Compliance Officers, and report to the Chief Compliance Officer.

  • Do you want to use your competencies in an international environment?
  • Do you enjoy combining independent monitoring with being pro-active and giving advice?
  • Do you like working in a high pace environment with great opportunities to influence the Compliance area of a Bank?

Then we are looking for you!

You will be part of Ikano Bank, where we create possibilities for a better living by providing banking and financial services. We do it with different perspectives, natural curiosity and long-term focus. A unique chance for greater responsibility and future opportunities. Let’s achieve great things together.

Help us simplify finance for the many people

The 2nd line Compliance Function is a vital independent function. The function’s role is to, with a risk-based approach, identify compliance gaps, recommend actions and support the Bank by giving advice and support. Play an active role in the maintenance and continuous improvement of the Bank’s compliance framework.

You will be based in Sweden, but your work arena will be all Ikano Bank’s branches, from your location preferably in Malmö.

Main Tasks As Compliance Officer

  • Identify, analyze and interpret and produce documentation on new external regulations that are relevant for the Bank’s licensed operation.
  • Independently assess compliance risks and thus contributing to the Compliance Function's overall risk assessment.
  • Perform monitoring and controls to secure the Bank’s adherence to external regulations. Examples of areas are Consumer Finance related regulations such as payment regulations, complaints, License Management, authority reporting, Customer protection and financial innovation regulations.
  • Produce reports based on the monitoring and control including recommendations based on the observations.
  • Provide advice, support and training on external requirements applicable to the Bank's licensed operations and ensure that governing documents within relevant areas are kept up to date.
  • Inform and train co-workers on new or changed external requirements and relevant internal regulations.
  • Regular written reporting to relevant stakeholders, including input to CEO and Board reporting.
  • Represent the Compliance Function on relevant meetings and committees.
  • Actively support the business in their work with compliance risk management and promote a strong Compliance culture
  • We are looking for a Compliance officer with a broad banking knowledge of regulations connected to our licensed operation. The areas AML and GDPR are already well covered in our team by your future colleagues.

We offer you

Operating at the center of events, you will have a very hands-on and active role, and create a great foundation for further development. You will get the chance to push boundaries and influence your daily work. It is essential that you enjoy working within a broad range of Compliance areas and countries and that you have a pro-active and “can-do”-approach, whilst maintaining an independent position. All as part of a caring and open culture where we live our values, work hard, have fun and operate with a long-term perspective.

Are you the one for Ikano?

  • You have a law degree (LL.M.) or equivalent
  • At least 5 years of relevant work experience from the financial sector within the Compliance area.
  • You have proven experience in interpreting external regulations applicable to a Swedish Bank.
  • You have the ability to produce professional written Compliance reports.
  • You are fluent in English and either Swedish, Danish or Norwegian.
  • You have an excellent ability to communicate and interact with people at all levels in the organization.
  • You have high integrity and are familiar with the 2nd line requirements.
  • You like to work independently, and you are proactive with an attitude to inspire and motivate people. It is essential that you are flexible end enjoy shifting between countries / areas / tasks.

Join us now – together we will find a better way

We will evaluate candidates and hold interviews on a continuous basis, last date to apply is September 21st.

About Ikano Bank (publ.)

Ikano Bank creates possibilities for better living by offering simple, fair, and affordable services, enabling a healthy economy for the many people and businesses. Its offer includes savings and loan products for consumers, sales support services for retailers, and leasing and factoring solutions for businesses. Ikano Bank operates in Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Norway, the UK, Poland, Germany and Austria, and is a part of Ingka Group. Ikano Bank’s head office is located in Malmö, Sweden and the company is registered in Älmhult, Sweden where the business was once founded.

companyDescription:
Transforming the invoice-to-cash process, Billogram’s intelligent platform 
orchestrates payments, strengthens customer relationships, and reduces churn. It delivers operational efficiency and a seamless payment experience that benefits businesses and their customers.
As we challenge the traditional world of invoicing, working with driven and talented people is a necessity and something Billogram has prioritized since the start. As of today, around 100 ambitious people make up our great team. 

The whole company is driven by a vision of building a service for the future and we have a great time doing so. To continue this journey, we are always looking for people who have new perspectives and like to keep reinventing what is already working.

If you're interested in getting to know us better, check out our career site - career.billogram.com
companySlogan: Easy payments. Quality engagement.
descriptionHtml:
At Billogram we like to be the good guys of billing, making money when clients are happy and pay on time rather than from missed payments. We believe it always pays off to treat people well. That’s why our platform for invoices and payments is built to remove pain, and instead be of gain to both our clients and their customers – improving their relations.


To support our growth and become a leader in the European consumer billing market we are looking for a Payments Support Administrator to join our Payments Support team.


About the role:

As a Payments Support Administrator, you will be a key person in our team, working at the intersection of payments, compliance, and process excellence. The role is a mix of administrative tasks and customer support, where your work has a direct impact on our financial integrity.


You will handle everything from payment allocations and refunds on our client funds account to monitoring transactions to ensure regulatory compliance. Your daily work will be in systems like Zendesk, CM1, and Notion, where you will independently collaborate with clients and their customers as well as other partners to resolve complex problems, and contribute to our internal knowledge base.


Primary responsibilities:

  • Own the payments flow by managing transactions, reconciling accounts, and ensuring compliance with financial regulations.
  • Keep accurate records and documentation related to payments and support cases to ensure transparency and compliance.
  • Analyze and act on transaction data to detect unexpected errors and signals of fraud or suspicious behavior.
  • Proactively drive improvements by identifying opportunities to enhance workflows and efficiency.
  • Collaborate closely with teams across Compliance, Product, and Finance to resolve complex payments and product-related issues.
  • Deliver high-quality support, simplifying complex payment-related topics and ensuring a seamless experience.
  • Stay updated on industry trends, regulations, and best practices to continuously refine how we handle payments and support.


What we are looking for:

You are meticulous and structured, with a strong sense of ownership. You ensure tasks are completed efficiently and accurately. Your ability to quickly notice when something seems off is a great asset, and you naturally take the initiative to investigate or escalate the situation to the right person. You thrive when presented with a complex administrative task and enjoy structuring out the work to get the result or answers necessary.


You are highly analytical and solution-oriented, and you see problems as an opportunity to learn and improve. You are a team player and thrive on collaboration, as you easily find win-win solutions by focusing on common goals.


Your communication is one of your key strengths, and you easily adapt your tone and message to the situation to ensure clarity. While administrative tasks are your core focus, you also appreciate occasional customer contact. You are curious and quick to learn new information, which drives you to continuously improve workflows and processes.


What will help you succeed:

  • At least three years of experience in administrative roles, such as back-office, payment operations, or similar roles involving structured tasks and documentation.
  • Experience working with financial transactions, KYC (Know Your Customer), or AML (Anti-Money Laundering) is a significant plus.
  • Ability to quickly learn and navigate administrative systems, including Zendesk, CM1 and Notion.
  • Fluency in Swedish and English, both written and spoken, is required. Fluency in German is a plus.


Why work with us?

At Billogram we promise you an exciting journey as we break into new markets and grow at a rapid pace. That aside you will get lots of freedom and opportunity to develop together with great colleagues at our office, right in the center of Stockholm. We also offer flexible working hours, exciting challenges and a whole lot of laughs.


We're committed to your long-term development, offering clear career progression paths. You'll also enjoy a fantastic benefits package, including 30 (+2) days of holiday, an occupational pension, a flexible wellness allowance, sick leave top-up, and parental leave compensation. At Billogram, our inclusive and diverse culture ensures that everyone can enjoy a fulfilling career and a great work-life balance.


At Billogram, we provide equal opportunities and strive for an inclusive and diverse environment. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to gender, transgender identity or expression, ethnicity, religion or other beliefs, disability, sexual orientation, or age.


Welcome to the world of Billogram!

companyDescription: Ikano Bank creates possibilities for better living by offering simple, fair, and affordable services, enabling a healthy economy for the many people and businesses. Its offer includes savings and loan products for consumers, sales support services for retailers, and leasing and factoring solutions for businesses. Ikano Bank operates in Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Norway, the UK, Poland, Germany and Austria, and is a part of Ingka Group. Ikano Bank’s head office is located in Malmö, Sweden and the company is registered in Älmhult, Sweden where the business was once founded..
companySlogan: Together, we create a great place to work where people are inspired to be the best they can be.
descriptionHtml:
As the B2B Fraud Excellence Specialist at Ikano Bank, you will play a crucial role in protecting the bank and its customers from fraud. You will be the organization’s subject matter expert in B2B fraud, with a key focus on the Swedish market.

Your daily work will involve both preventive actions and fraud investigations, ranging from simpler cases to more complex, high-risk matters. You will drive and implement preventive measures, ensuring consistent practices across the Nordic markets, and take ownership of regular reporting and analysis to monitor fraud trends and effectiveness of controls. You will collaborate closely with internal stakeholders in Commercial, Credit, AML & KYC as well as Collection to deliver a comprehensive fraud risk strategy with a commercial mindset. Maintaining a proactive and curious mindset, you will identify new tools and innovative methods to enhance the bank’s fraud prevention and detection capabilities, improving efficiency and effectiveness.

In This Role, Your Key Responsibilities Will Include

  • Managing daily fraud prevention activities and fraud investigations, ranging from minor to complex cases, with a focus on the Swedish market.
  • Developing and implementing new and effective controls across our Nordic markets to continuously strengthen the mitigation of fraud risks in B2B Leasing and Factoring.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally and across markets to ensure a holistic approach to fraud issues.
  • Providing training and guidance to colleagues and external partners on fraud-related matters.
  • Participate in and represent Fraud in various internal and external forums.
  • Keeping up-to-date with emerging fraud trends and threats, adapting strategies as needed to ensure the fraud risk remains within the bank’s risk appetite.

The role is based in Sundbyberg, Stockholm.

The last application date is September 14th.

About You

We are looking for a candidate with strong analytical skills and proven experience in one or more of the following areas: fraud prevention, late collections, or credit risk. Previous experience within the B2B leasing and/or factoring industry is considered a strong advantage.

You have an academic background in Business Administration, Criminology, or Law — or possess at least five years of equivalent and relevant professional experience.

You Bring

  • Proven experience in leading initiatives across markets.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
  • Fluency in Swedish and professional proficiency in English.

We Also See You As Someone Who

  • Is self-driven, proactive, and takes ownership.
  • Thrives in dynamic environments and manages multiple priorities effectively.
  • Has strong customer focus and understands the business context of fraud and/or credit risks.
  • Demonstrates excellent problem-solving and investigative skills.
  • Communicates clearly and confidently, both verbally and in writing.
  • Works collaboratively across functions and organizational boundaries.

We will place great emphasis on personal suitability, as well as your ability to work both independently and collaboratively across teams.

How To Apply

In this recruitment, Ikano Bank collaborates with Adecco. If you have any questions about the role or the recruitment process, you are welcome to contact the responsible recruiter: Marie Ekvall at marie.ekvall@adecco.se

We look forward to receiving your application!

Its offer includes savings and loan products for consumers, sales support services for retailers, and leasing and factoring solutions for businesses. Ikano Bank operates in Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Norway, the UK, Poland, Germany and Austria, and is a part of Ingka Group. Ikano Bank’s head office is located in Malmö, Sweden and the company is registered in Älmhult, Sweden where the business was once founded.

companyDescription:
Fastighetsbyrån är Sveriges marknadsledande mäklarfirma och förmedlar idag cirka var fjärde bostadsaffär i landet. Vi är över 1600 medarbetare på 250 kontor. Vår ambition är att fortsätta ta marknadsandelar och bli ännu fler. Vi är ständigt på jakt efter nya drivna och kunniga medarbetare.

Om vi tar vår position för given är vi illa ute. Framgången bygger till stor del på tanken om att vi aldrig blir fullärda. Och heller inte vill bli det. Vi strävar alltid efter att ligga i framkant och utveckla verksamheten med innovativa tjänster, verktyg och sätt att arbeta. Därför kan vi ge dig branschens bästa förutsättningar för en lyckad karriär.
companySlogan: Sveriges ledande mäklarföretag i Sverige, Spanien och Portugal!
descriptionHtml:
Om Fastighetsbyrån

Fastighetsbyrån ingår i Swedbank-koncernen och är en franchiseorganisation. Huvudkontorets uppgift är att utveckla branschens bästa franchisekoncept och stötta de lokala franchisetagarna.

På Huvudkontoret arbetar ca 50 personer som leder, driver och supportar en kedja med 1 700 medarbetare. Vi har det stora nöjet att kunna kalla oss för Sveriges ledande fastighetsmäklare, med drygt 285 kontor över hela landet och delar av Spanien och Portugal. Det gör oss störst både i omsättning och sett till antal affärer.

Om tjänsten

Vill du utveckla och jobba med Fastighetsbyråns interna compliance-arbete? Vi söker nu en senior jurist för tjänsten som Compliance Officer till Fastighetsbyråns huvudkontor i Stockholm. På uppdrag av bolagets styrelse kommer det att tillsättas en ny intern tjänst på huvudkontoret för att följa upp bolagets regelefterlevnad. Är du den vi söker erbjuds du en möjlighet att vara med och forma denna funktion i en händelserik miljö hos en arbetsgivare med mycket gott renommé och bra förmåner.

Dina Arbetsuppgifter Kommer Bland Annat Bestå Av

  • Ansvara för att bygga upp och utveckla bolagets Compliance funktion och dess arbetssätt.
  • Få en central roll i bolaget med övergripande ansvar för att leda, planera och driva Compliance funktionens aktiviteter i bolaget enligt styrelsens instruktion.
  • Bevaka nya och förändrade regelverk samt utbilda och stödja implementering av dessa i bolaget.
  • Genomföra översyn av centrala interna regelverk för Fastighetsbyrån.
  • Ge proaktivt råd och stöd till bolaget i deras dagliga verksamhet.
  • Genomföra riskanalyser avseende regelefterlevnadsområdet.
  • Löpande identifiera, kontrollera samt följa upp att verksamheten efterlever externa och interna regelverk.
  • Sammanställa och presentera rapportering till VD och styrelse.
  • Genomföra eventuellt utbyte och samarbete med moderbolaget Swedbank ABs Compliance funktion rörande koncerngemensamma metoder, system och processer.

I Denna Roll Behöver Du

  • Universitetsstudier i juridik.
  • Minst 5 års erfarenhet av compliancearbetet alternativt annan kontrollfunktion inom den finansiella sektorn (t.ex. bank, finansinstitut, tillsynsmyndighet, juridik/konsultföretag).
  • Ha hög integritet och förmåga att både utmana och stödja verksamheten i att förstå complianceriskerna.
  • Ett proaktivt och strukturerat arbetssätt samt en god kommunikationsförmåga både i tal och skrift.

Det Är Särskilt Meriterande Om Du

  • Har erfarenhet och kunskap från Fastighetsmäklarbranschen eller bolåneaffären.

Placeringsort

Fastighetsbyråns Huvudkontor i centrala Stockholm.

Övrigt

Vid eventuella frågor kontakta Chefsjurist Josefine Ekstrand på josefine.ekstrand@fastighetsbyran.se eller Rekryterings- och Employer Brandingansvarig Ulf Häger på ulf.hager@fastighetsbyran.se

Om Fastighetsbyrån

Vi har det stora nöjet att kunna kalla oss för Sveriges ledande fastighetsmäklare, med 285 kontor och cirka 1 700 medarbetare över hela landet och delar av Spanien och Portugal. Det gör oss störst både i omsättning och antal affärer. Och den som säljer mycket, kan också mycket. En erfarenhet som har hjälpt oss ta fram rätt tjänster och erbjudanden som hjälper såväl mäklaren som kunden i varje del av affären.

Att vår kunskap och erfarenhet uppskattas av många är tydligt; faktum är att vi förmedlar en fjärdedel av alla Sveriges bostadsaffärer. Det är ett förtroende vi är stolta över och värnar om varje dag – på varje kontor runt om i landet. För vi vill inte bara vara ledande i Sverige, vi strävar även efter att vara bäst på varje lokal ort och område. För det är där kunden bor och affären görs.

Som slutkandidat i Fastighetsbyråns rekryteringsprocess kan du komma att bli bakgrundskontrollerad förutsatt ditt samtycke. Så vänligen se över ditt CV innan ansökan.

2Secure är ett säkerhetsbolag och en internationell aktör inom bakgrundskontroller. 2Secure utför bland annat kontroller i samband med rekrytering, företagsförvärv, leverantörssamarbeten och ledningsprövningar.

Så vänligen se över ditt CV innan ansökan.2Secure är ett säkerhetsbolag och en internationell aktör inom bakgrundskontroller. 2Secure utför bland annat kontroller i samband med rekrytering, företagsförvärv, leverantörssamarbeten och ledningsprövningar.
title: Compliance Officer till Swedbank Fastighetsbyrå AB

companyDescription:
Scandinavian Trust offers Corporate Management and Secretarial services to local and foreign business owners. Our clients are international entrepreneurs and global businesses with local personnel, a branch or a holding company in Denmark, Finland, Norway and Sweden. 

Our key services include domiciliation and directorships, along with accounting and payroll. We also provide many other services such as management of bank relations, liquidations, management of audit relations, secretary services and agent assignments.
companySlogan: Let us manage your business in Scandinavia
descriptionHtml:
Head of Compliance – Stockholm


About Us

Scandinavian Trust is a boutique professional services firm with offices in Stockholm, Helsinki, Oslo, and Copenhagen. We provide clients with full Nordic coverage in corporate administration, financial services, and governance – always with a strong compliance culture at the core of our services.

Unlike the large global players, we are a close-knit firm where compliance is not only a control function but also part of how we deliver quality and trust to our clients. Here, your voice will be heard and your work will have direct impact – without the bureaucracy that often defines larger institutions.


The Role

We are now seeking a Head of Compliance to join our Stockholm office. This is a senior role reporting directly to the CEO, where you will take ownership of Scandinavian Trust’s compliance framework across all Nordic operations.

You will be responsible for AML, KYC, regulatory compliance, policies, and client agreements – ensuring that both we and our clients consistently meet the highest standards of integrity and regulatory requirements. The role also includes contract governance across the group, ensuring our client and vendor agreements are aligned with legal and regulatory expectations.


Key Responsibilities

  • Own and develop the company’s compliance framework, policies, and procedures across Sweden, Denmark, Norway, and Finland.
  • Oversee AML/KYC processes and act as the designated Compliance Officer towards relevant authorities.
  • Ensure GDPR and data protection compliance, including responsibility for client agreements and updates.
  • Take ownership of contract governance, including drafting, reviewing, and maintaining client and vendor agreements across the group.
  • Provide training and guidance to colleagues to strengthen the compliance culture across the firm.
  • Conduct compliance reviews and internal audits.
  • Liaise with regulators and external authorities when required.
  • Support client onboarding, including risk assessments and approval processes.
  • Drive the implementation of digital compliance solutions and explore the use of AI to enhance efficiency, monitoring, and reporting.


What We Offer

  • A senior position in a growing international boutique firm.
  • Direct reporting line to the CEO and a clear mandate to build and shape the compliance function.
  • An open and collaborative culture where compliance is seen as value creation, not bureaucracy.
  • A central Stockholm office combined with flexible working arrangements.
  • The opportunity to influence how we integrate digital tools and AI into compliance processes.
  • Full exposure to high-level client engagements across the Nordics.
  • A profitable and steadily growing firm with a strong and loyal client base, providing stability and long-term trust.


Who You Are

  • At least 5 years of relevant experience in compliance, legal, or risk management, ideally including leadership responsibility.
  • Experienced in AML, KYC, GDPR, and Nordic regulatory frameworks.
  • Desired experience with contract management, governance frameworks, and risk management.
  • Structured and detail-oriented, yet pragmatic in balancing compliance with business needs.
  • Strong communicator in English (Nordic language skills are a plus).
  • Motivated to take ownership and build scalable compliance processes in a fast-growing firm.


Why Join Scandinavian Trust?

We are a profitable and steadily growing firm with a strong and loyal client base. Our clients value our responsiveness, professionalism, and ability to handle complex cross-border assignments. This creates an environment of stability and long-term trust – the perfect foundation for building a compliance function with real impact. At the same time, our culture is professional yet friendly, and we take pride in being a workplace where people enjoy working together and growing together.



Interested? Let’s Talk!

For questions about the role, please contact:

Lars-Andreas Nilsen

CEO

+47 48 26 26 21

andreas@scandinaviantrust.no



We look forward to hearing from you!

descriptionHtml:
AcadeMedia är med sina närmare 21 000 medarbetare och 200 000 elever Europas största utbildningsföretag med verksamheter i Sverige, Norge, Finland, Tyskland, Nederländerna, Storbritannien och Polen. Vår storlek skapar trygghet och ger oss förutsättningar att vara en långsiktig och stabil utbildningsaktör som bidrar till det gemensamma samhällsbygget. AcadeMedia drivs av människor som arbetar för att skapa ett bättre och mer hållbart samhälle, med utbildning och lärande som grund. Vår vision är att leda utvecklingen av framtidens utbildning och tillsammans rustar vi våra barn, elever och vuxenstuderande med kunskaper och bildning samtidigt som vi ger dem förutsättningar att kunna forma sin egen framtid. Vi är övertygade om att utbildning kan skapa förändring på riktigt, både för enskilda individer och för hela samhällen – vi kallar det ”ChangeThrough Education”.


Vill du vara med och skapa något nytt inom regelefterlevnad i en föränderlig miljö med varierande arbetsuppgifter och med ett stort fokus från verksamheten och styrelsen?


Om rollen

Detta är en ny roll där fokus kommer att vara att identifiera och granska regulatoriska risker inom i första hand AcadeMediakoncernens svenska verksamhet samt ge stöd till verksamheterna i frågor rörande regelefterlevnad. AcadeMedia har ett viktigt samhällsuppdrag som Sveriges och Europas största och ledande utbildningsföretag. För att uppfylla de krav som ställs på ett börsnoterat bolag med ett viktigt samhällsuppdrag söker AcadeMedia nu en Compliance Officer. I uppdraget ingår att sätta den centrala strukturen för compliancearbetet på ett sätt som tillåter korrekt och effektiv stöttning till den operativa verksamheten. Rollen omfattar arbetsuppgifter såsom analys, granskning, utvärdering och rådgivning inom rådande regelverk. I rollen kan personalansvar komma att ingå.


I tjänsten ingår vidare att:

  • Löpande följa upp verksamhetens regelefterlevnad och genomföra kontroller
  • Ansvara för att säkerställa att koncernen har goda riskhanteringsprocesser
  • Arbeta tillsammans med koncernstaber såsom ekonomi och IT samt verksamheterna i att utveckla koncernens riskmatris och intern kontroll
  • Ansvara för koncernens riskrapporteringsprocess
  • Ansvara för att säkerställa att koncernen har god avtals- och regelefterlevnad och att dessa processer har ett tydligt ägarskap i linjen samt att de utvecklas löpande
  • Identifiera och värdera regulatoriska risker inom hela AcadeMedia-koncernen samt utarbeta policys och instruktioner
  • Ge råd och stöd till samt utbilda och informera AcadeMediakoncernen inom compliancefunktionens ansvarsområde
  • Omvärldsbevakning gällande nya och förändrade regelverk, framförallt inom svensk skola


Du kommer att rapportera till chefsjuristen och ingå i AcadeMedias centrala stödfunktioner. Som Compliance Officer kommer du löpande informera AcadeMedias koncernledning och styrelse om regulatoriska risker som kan uppstå inom koncernen och föreslå lösningar för att hindra/förekomma incidenter. Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning på heltid med placering i Stockholm. Resor i tjänsten kan förekomma. Tillträde enligt överenskommelse.


Vem söker vi?

För att lyckas i den här rollen krävs en stor passion för ämnet. Du jobbar självständigt, metodiskt, strukturerat och med eget driv men förstår samtidigt vikten av organisation och struktur. Du är analytisk och noggrann och att du uppskattar god kommunikation och samarbete. Vi söker dig som utstrålar tydlighet och kombinerar ödmjuk pondus med utmärkt kommunikativ förmåga. Vidare trivs du i en dynamisk och komplex miljö full av kontakter, förändring, utveckling och med en samhällsnyttig inriktning. Rätt person för rollen är redo att ta ett helhetsansvar för området compliance och stå på egna ben.


Vi söker dig med:

  • Juridisk examen eller motsvarande
  • Mycket god erfarenhet av praktiskt compliancearbete som konsult eller på bolag eller myndighet
  • Väldigt god förståelse och praktisk erfarenhet av tillämpningen av de regelverk som styr compliancefunktionens arbete
  • Flytande svenska och engelska i både tal och skrift
  • Meriterande med relevant erfarenhet från skola/vård/omsorg eller annat tjänsteföretag som påverkas starkt av regelverk och externa intressenters intryck/uppfattning


Du välkomnas till en dynamisk och trivsam arbetsmiljö med drivna och engagerade kollegor. Du välkomnas till en dynamisk och trivsam arbetsmiljö med drivna och engagerade kollegor. Vi erbjuder bland annat kollektivavtalade förmåner och försäkringar samt friskvårdsbidrag.



Frågor och ansökan

I den här rekryteringen har AcadeMedia valt att samarbeta med Constatera Search & Interim. För frågor kontakta rekryteringskonsult Lotta Belfrage Palmqvist, lotta.belfrage-palmqvist@constatera.se, 0722-522 373.


Ansök snarast med CV och brev via www.constatera.se. Rollen är sökbar så länge annonsen är publicerad på nätet. Urval och intervjuer sker under andra halva av augusti 2025.


Vi ber dig svara kortfattat och beskrivande på följande frågor i ditt ansökningsbrev:

1. Varför är du intresserad av rollen och AcadeMedia?

2. Hur matchar du kravprofilen i annonsen (konkretisera gärna dina matchande erfarenheter och kompetenser)?


Välkommen med din ansökan!

companyDescription:
Jurek är ett rekryterings- och konsultföretaget som präglas av framåtanda.

Vi lägger mycket energi på att omsorgsfullt identifiera och matcha rätt kandidat till rätt uppdrag. Att gå på djupet "utanför CV:et"​ för att matcha rätt mellan kandidaten och det specifika uppdragets kultur och vision är vår absoluta ambition. Det värde vi lyckas leverera varje gång är också en av förklaringarna till att våra kunder återkommer gång på gång.
 
Vår affärsidé är att skapa en win-win situation för båda våra intressenter, d v s arbetstagare och uppdragsgivare. Detta uppnår vi genom att leverera flexibla och kundanpassade rekryterings- och bemanningslösningar över hela landet.
 
Vår vision är att bli det ledande företaget inom vår bransch och nisch, främst genom nöjda kunder och kandidater som hjälper oss att expandera!
companySlogan: Vi hittar, skapar & matchar talang!
descriptionHtml:
Detta är en intresseanmälan för dig som vill skapa kontakt för kommande spännande uppdrag inom AML/KYC-området. Vi arbetar löpande med denna typ av uppdrag och söker ständigt efter talanger på området. Är du en driven och analytisk person med några års erfarenhet inom AML/KYC? Vill du bidra till att förebygga finansiell brottslighet och arbeta i en dynamisk miljö? Då vill vi gärna höra från dig!


Om rollen:

Vi på Jurek söker kontinuerligt efter talangfulla AML/KYC-analytiker för våra kunders räkning. Arbetsuppgifterna kan variera beroende på uppdragets längd och kundens behov, men inkluderar vanligtvis:

• Insamling, undersökning och analys av kundaktiviteter och beteenden

• Skapande och uppdatering av kundprofiler enligt KYC-krav

• Analys av potentiella risker inom AML- och KYC-området

• Leverans av högkvalitativa granskningar av kunder

• Bidrag till förbättring av AML/KYC-processer

Utöver detta kan arbetet även innebära interna kontroller (stickprov), bidrag till den allmänna riskbedömningen, myndighetsrapportering, hantering av transaktionsmonitorering, ODD samt PEP/RCA och sanktionskontroll.

Detta rör inte en specifik roll utan fungerar som en intresseanmälan för dig som vill arbeta på uppdrag inom AML/KYC framåt. Som konsult inom AML/KYC kommer dina arbetsuppgifter att variera beroende på uppdrag och roll.


Vem är du?

För att lyckas i denna roll behöver du vara ambitiös, målinriktad och ha ett starkt driv att uppnå resultat. Du är analytisk och har ett riskbaserat tänkesätt, vilket hjälper dig att identifiera och hantera potentiella risker i AML/KYC-processer. Din förmåga att leverera en förstklassig kundupplevelse är en av dina starkaste sidor, och du trivs i en miljö där kvalitet och noggrannhet är avgörande.

Vi söker dig som:

• Har några års tidigare erfarenhet från AML/KYC-arbete

• Har en akademisk utbildning inom relevant område

• Är analytisk och har ett riskbaserat tänkesätt

• Trivs i en miljö där stora krav ligger på noggrannhet

• Är flytande i svenska och engelska


Om oss:

Jurek Recruitment & Consulting grundades 2006 och är ett växande företag med fokus på flexibla och kundanpassade rekryterings- och konsulttjänster. Vi rekryterar och hyr ut talanger inom en mängd områden, från studenter till erfarna akademiker, med specialistkompetens. Våra uppdrag spänner över områdena juridik, ekonomi, HR, marknad/kommunikation, administration och management. Då varje uppdrag är unikt arbetar vi för att alltid skapa en perfect match för våra konsulter och kunder.

Konsult hos Jurek

År 2024 utsågs vi till en av Sveriges 100 mest attraktiva arbetsgivare av Karriärföretagen. Det är fint att få ett kvitto på vårt viktiga arbete med att erbjuda unika karriär- och utvecklingsmöjligheter. Som konsult hos oss får du chansen att arbeta på spännande arbetsplatser inom olika branscher och vid din sida har du alltid en dedikerad konsultchef som stöttar dig under ditt uppdrag och hjälper dig framåt i din karriär. Du kommer dessutom bjudas in på flera spännande nätverksträffar och aktiviteter tillsammans med konsultchefer och övriga konsulter på Jurek

Nyfiken på att veta mer om hur det är att konsulta via oss? Spana in vad våra konsulter säger här: https://jurek.se/konsult-hos-oss-pa-jurek
