I rollen har du ansvar för ordning och reda samt kvalitén i den löpande affären med fokus på administration av kundärenden, cash management samt KYC (kundkännedom).Tjänsten innebär att du bland annat:arbetar proaktivt med service till våra företagskunderföreslår och genomför affärer i direktkontakt med kund och i samråd med kundansvarig företagsrådgivareär en central del i KYC arbetet, där du genomför KYC samt har en kontakt med KYC & AML-gruppen i kundkännedomsfrågorplanerar kundärenden tillsammans med företagsrådgivareDin profilDu tycker om att arbeta strukturerat och målinriktat med kunden i centrum och som person är du en utåtriktad och initiativrik person med förmåga att se till helheten. Du trivs med att skapa och utveckla relationer både internt och externt, att arbeta i team är en naturlig del för dig. För att lyckas i rollen krävs hög administrativ förmåga och god ordning och reda.Du behöver ha ett starkt intresse för företagsaffären, det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av arbete med företagskunder samt om du har Swedsec rådgivarlicens. Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper och hur du med dessa kompletterar vårt företagsteam.Välkommen med din ansökan!Skicka din ansökan senast 17 september och berätta varför du söker och vad du kan bidra med i rollen och till Sparbanken Skåne. Personprofilstester och bakgrundskontroller ingår i rekryteringsprocessen. Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning, provanställning kan komma att tillämpas.Har du frågor om tjänsten?Kontakta rekryterande chef Ola Sandberg, Företagschef, tel. 046 - 37 19 83Kontaktperson för Finansförbundet är Samet Elezi, tel. 046 - 35 06 54Kontaktperson för SACO är Torbjörn Helldén, telefon 046 - 37 18 11Som medarbetare på Sparbanken Skåne är du med och bidrar till att utveckla samhället, förverkliga drömmar och hjälpa människor att växa. Genom att låta delar av vinsten gå till lokala projekt inom idrott, kultur, utbildning och näringsliv bidrar vi till att skapa framtidstro där du bor. Detta gör oss stolta! Läs mer om oss på www.sparbankenskane.se
title: Sparbanken Skåne söker Kundstöd Företag till Löddeköpinge

companyDescription:
Skånemejerier är det ledande mejeriföretaget i södra Sverige, en hungrig utmanare nationellt och dessutom en del av världens ledande mejerikoncern Lactalis.

Hos oss samlas ambitiösa, pragmatiska och engagerade medarbetare som alla jobbar mot samma mål – att förse svenska folket med riktigt bra mat. I vårt sortiment finns närproducerade mejerivaror, Sveriges mest köpta juice och ostspecialiteter från Sverige och Europa.

Vår vision är att bli Sveriges mest hållbara mejeri, och då handlar det inte bara om att erbjuda produkter som är bra för både människa och miljö, utan också om att vara en hållbar arbetsplats. Vi värnar om gott ledarskap, givande samarbeten och en välkomnande och inkluderande arbetsmiljö. Hos oss får du möjlighet att utvecklas, växa och påverka vår gemensamma väg framåt.

Välkommen till Skånemejerier!
companySlogan: Vi ger näring åt framtiden, genom att producera riktigt bra mat som både smakar och gör gott.
descriptionHtml:
Skånemejerier är det ledande mejeriföretaget i södra Sverige, en utmanare nationellt och dessutom en del av världens största mejerikoncern Lactalis.


Vägledda av ambition, engagemang och enkelhet arbetar alla våra medarbetare mot samma mål – att utveckla och producera riktigt bra mat som både smakar och gör gott.


Vår vision är att bli Sveriges mest hållbara mejeri, och då handlar det inte bara om att erbjuda produkter som är bra för både människa och miljö, utan också om att vara en hållbar arbetsplats. Hos oss får du möjlighet att utvecklas, växa och påverka vår gemensamma väg framåt. Nu söker vi en Regulatory & Compliance Manager till vårt huvudkontor i Malmö!



Arbetsbeskrivning


I rollen som Regulatory & Compliance Manager kommer du att ansvara för att säkerställa att vår verksamhet och produkter följer gällande livsmedelslagstiftning och förpackningskrav. Rollen omfattar även bredare compliance-arbete inkluderande frågor inom dataskydd och anti-korruption och omvärldsbevakning samt att driva implementering av nya lag- och regelkrav inom organisationen. Du rapporterar till vår Legal Manager och arbetar nära flera funktioner inom bolaget.


Dina huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter inkluderar följande:


-Livsmedelslagstiftning och förpackning

Säkerställa efterlevnad av nationell/EU-lagstiftning och interna riktlinjer gällande livsmedel, märkning, innehållsdeklarationer och förpackning. Koordinera tvärfunktionellt arbete och vara kontaktperson mot myndigheter. Agera stöd vid certifieringar och revisioner.


-Compliance (GDPR, anti-korruption etc.)

Implementera och underhålla rutiner för GDPR, anti-korruption och andra compliance-områden. Identifiera och hantera risker, granska uppförandekoder och stödja organisationen i regelefterlevnad.


-Lagbevakning och implementering

Bevaka och analysera lagstiftningsförändringar. Driva anpassningsprojekt och samordna företagets lagforum.


-Policyarbete, utbildning och rådgivning

Utveckla policies och riktlinjer. Planera och genomföra utbildningar för att stärka regelefterlevnad och riskmedvetenhet. Ge expertstöd till olika funktioner vid produktlanseringar, förpackningsval och konsumentkommunikation.


Vem är du?


Vi söker dig som har flera års erfarenhet av arbete med regelefterlevnad inom livsmedelsbranschen eller liknande reglerad verksamhet. Du har en akademisk utbildning inom juridik, livsmedelsteknik och/eller annan relevant utbildning. Du har god kunskap om nationell och EU-lagstiftning, särskilt inom livsmedel och förpackning. Du är van att tolka och implementera nya regelverk i praktiken och har erfarenhet av projektledning, hålla i utbildningar samt policyutveckling. Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av att arbeta med dataskydd och andra compliance-områden, såsom anti-korruption. Du har en utmärkt förmåga att uttrycka dig i tal och skrift såväl på svenska som engelska.


Som person är du kommunikativ och har en god samarbetsförmåga. Du har en god förmåga att arbeta självständigt och strukturerat. Du har ett proaktivt förhållningssätt och trivs med att arbeta i en komplex verksamhet. Dessutom är du nyfiken på att utvecklas och lära dig nytt och har ett starkt driv framåt.


Vad erbjuder vi dig?


På Skånemejerier utvecklar vi ständigt vår verksamhet och därför behöver vi drivna och nyfikna medarbetare som vågar testa nya vägar, gillar att arbeta tillsammans med andra och har drivkraften att påverka vilka vägar vi tar. Eftersom Skånemejerier är en del av Lactalis Group som har produktion i ett femtiotal länder på fyra kontinenter ger det våra medarbetare tillgång till en stor bredd av kompetensutveckling och möjligheten att bygga internationella kontaktnätverk.


Skånemejeriers vision är att bli Sveriges mest hållbara mejeri. Här är värnandet om våra medarbetare en grundbult. Vi vill ha en kultur som präglas av arbetsglädje och att nå resultat och som vägledning har vi värderingarna ambition, engagemang och enkelhet. Vi strävar efter gemensamma och individuella prestationer som utvecklar både företaget och människor. Vi agerar som entreprenörer, är ansvarstagande och lojala och vi är tillgängliga, prestigelösa, öppna och pragmatiska.


Skånemejerier har blivit utsett till ett av Sveriges Karriärföretag 2025. Läs mer om verksamheten här: Hem - Skånemejerier Företagssite


Ansökan


I denna rekryteringsprocess samarbetar Lactalis & Skånemejerier med Jurek Recruitment & Consulting. Då urvalsprocessen sker löpande så ser vi gärna att du skickar in din ansökan så snart som möjligt. Vänligen notera att vi inte tar emot ansökningar via email utan ansökan sker via Jureks hemsida www.jurek.se. För frågor om tjänsten är du varmt välkommen att kontakta ansvarig rekryteringskonsult Linn Marthinussen på telefon: +46 76 002 69 41 alternativt via email linn.marthinussen@jurek.se

Vi strävar efter gemensamma och individuella prestationer som utvecklar både företaget och människor. Vi agerar som entreprenörer, är ansvarstagande och lojala och vi är tillgängliga, prestigelösa, öppna och pragmatiska.Skånemejerier har blivit utsett till ett av Sveriges Karriärföretag 2025. Läs mer om verksamheten här: https://foretag.skanemejerier.se/AnsökanI denna rekryteringsprocess samarbetar Lactalis & Skånemejerier med Jurek Recruitment & Consulting. Då urvalsprocessen sker löpande så ser vi gärna att du skickar in din ansökan så snart som möjligt. Vänligen notera att vi inte tar emot ansökningar via email utan ansökan sker via Jureks hemsida www.jurek.se. För frågor om tjänsten är du varmt välkommen att kontakta ansvarig rekryteringskonsult Linn Marthinussen på telefon: +46 76 002 69 41 alternativt via email linn.marthinussen@jurek.se

companyDescription:
We are a single-source financial solutions provider dedicated to driving positive change while supporting the growth and ambitions of asset managers, allocators, financial institutions, and family offices around the world. 

Established in Bermuda in 2003, we have continually disrupted the industry through our investment in innovation and talent. Today, we set the pace in fund and asset servicing and stand out for our unique single-source solution and unified cross asset-class platform which supports the entire value chain, harnesses leading innovative technology, and benefits from cross-jurisdictional expertise delivered by a long-standing management team and over 13,000 highly integrated professionals.   

As a pioneering data and fintech-enabled company, we are a disruptor driving digital tools into fund and asset servicing. However, our vision to drive positive change extends beyond the industry. The Apex Foundation, a not-for-profit entity, is our passionate commitment to empower sustainable change.
descriptionHtml:
The Apex Group was established in Bermuda in 2003 and is now one of the world’s largest fund administration and middle office solutions providers.

Our business is unique in its ability to reach globally, service locally and provide cross-jurisdictional services. With our clients at the heart of everything we do, our hard-working team has successfully delivered on an unprecedented growth and transformation journey, and we are now represented by over circa 13,000 employees across 112 offices worldwide.Your career with us should reflect your energy and passion.

That’s why, at Apex Group, we will do more than simply ‘empower’ you. We will work to supercharge your unique skills and experience.

Take the lead and we’ll give you the support you need to be at the top of your game. And we offer you the freedom to be a positive disrupter and turn big ideas into bold, industry-changing realities.

For our business, for clients, and for you

Fund Accounting AVP

The APEX Group is looking for an AVP Fund Accountant to join our inclusive and collaborative team in Stockholm. This full-time Senior Fund Administrator role offers an attractive salary and benefits package.

You will work in a team to manage a portfolio of allocated clients and the PE/RE/Credit-funds they are managing. Your role is pivotal in ensuring the prompt and precise administration of all the funds. Proficiency in both Swedish and English is a prerequisite for this position.

Key Responsibilities

  • Administration of VC, PE, RE, Infrastructure or/and Credit funds.
  • Project management and on-boarding new funds.
  • Calculation and processing of fund capital calls and distributions.
  • Waterfall calculations
  • Quarterly Investor Reports and Capital Account Statements.
  • Managing fund closings, equalizations and re-balancing between fund entities after closings.
  • Investor onboarding including AML/KYC documents review.
  • Regulatory reporting including Annex IV, FATCA/CRS, AML/KYC.
  • Assist in staff collaboration and ensure that client deliverables are being met.
  • Regular interaction with clients, client service managers, fund accountants and other relevant parties.
  • Engaging with fund managers to ensure seamless communication, handling ad-hoc questions and swift resolution of any issues.


    • Skills / experience:

    • A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Finance, Economics or equivalent.
    • At least five year of experience from fund administration or similar role.
    • A detailed understanding of Alternative Investments, Private Equity and other close ended fund structures and the fund administration industry.
    • Knowledge of the local regulatory environment.
    • Experience using Microsoft Office, particularly Excel.
    • Experience with Fortnox, Capego, Allvue, eFront and/or Navision is a plus.
    • A methodical and analytical approach to tasks.
    • Exceptional communication skills, both verbal and written, and the ability to collaborate effectively within a team.
    • A commitment to meeting deadlines and the capacity to manage time effectively.
    • An ability to multi-task and manage projects.
    • Excellent interpersonal skills to develop working relationships with colleagues, clients, other stake holders.

    Our Commitment to You:

    At APEX Group, we recognize that our people are our most valuable resource. We are dedicated to fostering talent through a variety of educational support, sponsorships, and in-house training initiatives. By joining our global network, you will have access to opportunities that will support your career growth and professional aspirations.

    As a truly global business, we tailor benefits to each location to safeguard our people and respect local cultures and ways of working. Our people are our greatest asset, and we invest in talent development. Join our global network, and you will benefit from education support, sponsorship, and in-house training programs.

    Please don't hesitate to apply with Apex Group today if this full-time job in Stockholm sounds like a great fit for you. We would love to help you get your next role and fulfil your professional ambitions.

    Apex Group Ltd ("Apex" or "the Group"), established in Bermuda in 2003, is a global financial services provider. With over 80 offices worldwide and 13,000 employees in 38 countries, Apex delivers an expansive range of services to asset managers, financial institutions, private clients, and family offices. Apex's purpose is to be more than just a financial services provider. The Group is committed to driving positive change to address three core areas: the environment and climate change, women's empowerment and economic independence, and education and social mobility. Life at Apex isn't just about the work you do. It's about embracing the culture and loving what you do. Every employee plays a part in making Apex who we are today, and the more we grow, the more important that becomes. The international nature of our business and global network of offices means that there are opportunities to broaden your life experiences and take short-term or permanent relocation options.

    Disclaimer: Unsolicited CVs sent to Apex (Talent Acquisition Team or Hiring Managers) by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this position. Apex operates a direct sourcing model and where agency assistance is required, the Talent Acquisition team will engage directly with our exclusive recruitment partners.

companyDescription:
Verdane is a specialist growth buyout investment firm that partners with tech-enabled and sustainable businesses that help to digitalise and decarbonise the European economy. Verdane funds have the most flexible mandates in the industry. The firm can invest as a minority or majority investor, replacement or growth capital, in single companies or in portfolios of companies.

Verdane has over €8 billion in assets under management and its funds have made more than 200 investments in fast-growing businesses since 2003. Verdane’s team of over 150 investment professionals and operating experts is based out of Berlin, Munich, Copenhagen, Helsinki, London, Oslo and Stockholm, and combines deep sector expertise with deep local networks and presence in core European markets.

Verdane is also a certified B Corporation, the most ambitious sustainability accreditation globally. The firm only backs businesses that pass its 2040 test, which indicates whether the company can thrive in a more sustainable future economy.

Verdane is partly owned by the Verdane Foundation, which is focused on two areas: climate change and more equitable and inclusive local communities.

Further information: www.verdane.com
companySlogan: The preferred growth partner to ambitious companies that will thrive in a sustainable world.
descriptionHtml:
Job description

We are looking for an ambitious part-time Financial Controller Intern (20 %) with a strong drive. As an Intern at Verdane you will be a part of our finance department which currently has 13 members. The Department is organized in three segments: fund controlling, controlling, and accounting. The Financial Controller Intern will first and foremost be working closely with the controlling-team but should expect to be involved with other segments as well.

You will be involved in day-to-day controlling activities within an international growth equity investment firm. You will be working closely with experienced colleagues which will be contributing with academic development relevant for your studies. The ideal candidate is a smart and structured problem-solver who thrives well in an entrepreneurial environment.

Your responsibilities

  • Review expenses and approve invoices.
  • Support the payroll process.
  • Assist in the preparation and creation of quarterly financial reports.
  • Support the KYC team
  • Participate in various projects within the finance function



Your profile

  • Currently pursuing a degree in the fields of auditing or accounting, preferably on your 2nd or 3rd year in your degree.
  • Previus internships experience within relevant fields such as controlling or accounting is a plus
  • Beyond traditional tools like Excel we also expect interns to be comfortable leveraging AI-driven platforms for research, analysis, and productivity. Familiarity with modern data tools and a willingness to adopt emerging technologies are essential.
  • You are comfortable with written and spoken english and Swedish



Our corporate values

  • We dare to be different: We are bold in our thinking and challenge the status quo
  • We get things done: We actively work to shorten decision making cycles, always completing our set tasks with quality
  • We love what we do: We are genuinely committed and passionately curious, entrepreneurial in spirit and deed – the best idea wins
  • We respect people and the planet: We show responsibility and aspire to be on the right side of history, helping to move our industry towards more sustainable, long-term thinking



Why us?

We offer an exciting opportunity to join a purpose-driven, international investment firm with a strong culture and commitment to excellence. As part of the finance team, you will gain insight into the operational engine of a leading growth equity firm, with opportunities to grow and influence.

Please upload your CV. The team will evaluate applicants continuously and we encourage candidates to apply as soon as you feel ready.

Verdane is committed to creating a diverse, equitable, and inclusive working environment and we actively seek qualified candidates regardless of background, gender identity, orientation, or ethnicity.

About us

Verdane is a specialist growth buyout investment firm that partners with tech-enabled and sustainable businesses that help to digitalise and decarbonise the European economy. Verdane funds have the most flexible mandates in the industry. The firm can invest as a minority or majority investor, replacement or growth capital, in single companies or in portfolios of companies. Verdane has over €8 billion in assets under management and its funds have made more than 200 investments in fast-growing businesses since 2003. Verdane’s team of over 150 investment professionals and operating experts is based out of Berlin, Munich, Copenhagen, Helsinki, London, Oslo and Stockholm, and combines deep sector expertise with deep local networks and presence in core European markets. Verdane is also a certified B Corporation, the most ambitious sustainability accreditation globally. The firm only backs businesses that pass its 2040 test, which indicates whether the company can thrive in a more sustainable future economy. Verdane is partly owned by the Verdane Foundation, which is focused on two areas: climate change and more equitable and inclusive local communities.

companyDescription: Svalner Skatt & Transaktion is Sweden's leading independent tax advisory firm and is through its active membership in WTS Global part of the largest independent international tax network in the world. By launching financial transaction services in October 2019, Svalner is now taking the step to become the first independent integrated tax and transaction advisory firm in the Nordics. Svalner is a tax and transaction advisory firm providing corporate clients with high end tax and transaction advice. The services are focused on transaction tax services, e.g. purchase/vendor tax due diligences and tax structuring. The membership in WTS Global enables Svalner further to offer cross border assignments as an integral part of the range of services offered.
descriptionHtml:
Vill du arbeta i en roll där du blir en nyckelperson i det dagliga ekonomiarbetet? Välkommen att arbeta med Sveriges ledande och Nordens första oberoende specialister inom skatt, finansiell transaktionsrådgivning, ESG, fondadministration och redovisning.

Nu söker vi en ekonomiassistent som vill ta ansvar, bidra till Svalners fortsatta tillväxtresa och spela en central roll i vårt ekonomiteam. Rollen är baserad på Svalners kontor i centrala Stockholm.

Om rollen

Dina arbetsuppgifter kommer bland annat vara:

  • Helhetsansvar över leverantörsreskontra vilket innefattar kontering, registrering och betalningar
  • Kundreskontra, vilket innefattar fakturering, inbetalningar, påminnelser, kravhantering och vidarefakturering
  • Medverka vid månadsbokslut och årsbokslut samt balansavstämningar
  • Registrering av nya klienter och ärenden samt kundkännedom (KYC)
  • Utskick av uppdragsbrev/avtal
  • Utläggshantering – kontering och utbetalningar

Vi söker dig som

  • Har några års erfarenhet av liknande arbetsuppgifter
  • Har någon form av eftergymnasial utbildning inom ekonomi
  • Har ett system- och digitaliseringsintresse samt en stark vilja att utveckla rutiner och processer
  • Är positiv, proaktiv, noggrann, ansvarsfull och har en god analytisk förmåga
  • Har goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift
  • Har grundläggande kunskaper i Excel och Officepaketet

Din ansökan bör innehålla CV, personligt brev, betyg från gymnasium och universitet samt eventuella andra intyg.

Välkommen med din ansökan!

Vid frågor kontakta Mathilda Hagerborg.

Om Svalner

 Svalner, en del av Svalner Atlas Group, är Sveriges ledande och Nordens första oberoende specialister inom skatt, finansiell transaktionsrådgivning och redovisning. Med specialistkompetens och ett unikt och starkt fokus på klientnytta kan vi erbjuda knivskarp och snabbfotad expertis inom skatterådgivning, transaktionsrådgivning, redovisning, ESG och fund services. Svalner Atlas Group finns i Sverige, Holland, Finland, Norge och Danmark med 370 medarbetare. I Sverige är vi över 170 medarbetare och vi befinner oss i en spännande tillväxtfas med många spännande satsningar. Med rätt inställning och engagemang ser vi inga gränser för dina karriärmöjligheter hos oss på Svalner.

companyDescription:
Get insights from Accenture in Denmark, Finland, Norway and Sweden.

About Accenture
 
Accenture is a leading global professional services company that helps the world’s leading businesses, governments and other organizations build their digital core, optimize their operations, accelerate revenue growth and enhance citizen services—creating tangible value at speed and scale. We are a talent- and innovation-led company with approximately 791,000 people serving clients in more than 120 countries. Technology is at the core of change today, and we are one of the world’s leaders in helping drive that change, with strong ecosystem relationships. We combine our strength in technology and leadership in cloud, data and AI with unmatched industry experience, functional expertise and global delivery capability. Our broad range of services, solutions and assets across Strategy & Consulting, Technology, Operations, Industry X and Song, together with our culture of shared success and commitment to creating 360° value, enable us to help our clients reinvent and build trusted, lasting relationships. We measure our success by the 360° value we create for our clients, each other, our shareholders, partners and communities. Visit us at accenture.com
companySlogan: Let There Be Change
descriptionHtml:
There is never a typical day at Accenture, but that is why we love it here! This is an extraordinary opportunity to build a rewarding career at Accenture Banking Technology Unit. Working in highly collaborative cross-border teams for world-leading clients, we will nurture your talent in an inclusive culture that values diversity.

Whatever your aspirations are, make the most of them in a career with Accenture. Whether you are a strategic thinker, a digital innovator, a tech wizard, or a business problem solver, you will find an exciting career path on the cutting edge. We would love to get to know your unique blend of talents and help you explore and grow them further.

Accenture Banking Technology Unit is part of the Accenture’s global capability network and we serve several clients in Nordics and globally. Banking Technology is in the intersection of business and technology, and we have pure techies and more business oriented people in the team. You’ll get access to Accenture’s vast knowledge base and experienced colleagues to help you to grow to next level on your professional career.

Role Description

Are you passionate about using technology to combat financial crime and make a real difference in the banking sector? We are seeking talented Financial Crime Prevention Banking Technology Consultants at all levels to join our dynamic team.

As an FCP Banking Technology Consultant with us, you’ll have the opportunity to work closely with leading banks in the Nordics, helping them safeguard their operations from financial crime risks. You’ll be part of or lead project teams that drive impactful solutions, support regulatory compliance, and foster client success.

Your specific role and responsibilities will be tailored to each engagement, allowing you to bring your expertise where it’s needed most and develop your skills in a challenging and rewarding environment.

Responsibilities:


  • Drive technical transformation to align with our clients' business goals and compliance processes.
  • Contribute to solution design, system integration, and implementation of leading vendor platforms and in-house solutions.
  • Serve as a functional and technical expert in financial crime prevention for client engagements.
  • Apply strong problem-solving, analytical, and functional expertise to address complex challenges in financial crime prevention


    • Professional Requirements:


  • Master or Bachelor’s degree from a technical or business school
  • You would have a minimum of 3-5 years of experience within technology implementations and advisory in financial crime prevention including AML, transaction monitoring, sanctions, KYC and fraud.
  • Hands-on experience with one or more leading financial crime technology solutions (e.g., NICE Actimize, Oracle FCCM, SAS, FICO TONBELLER, Pega, Quantexa, or similar).
  • Understanding of data management, ETL, and analytics tools, experience with SQL and Python is a plus.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • High drive, entrepreneurial attitude and good interpersonal skills
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills in English; proficiency in Swedish is highly advantageous
  • Resident in Sweden


    • What is in it for you:


  • We believe in bringing the best to our clients through continuous learning & improvement – from basic skills to industry-specific content
  • Mentoring and continuous evaluation that ensure your career continues to progress
  • We offer a performance management process that focuses on your strengths, progress and career possibilities
  • Flexible work arrangements for maintaining work-life balance
  • Extensive wellbeing program, that boosts your overall wellbeing, energy and ability to work in a sustainable way
  • Opportunities to get involved in corporate citizenship initiatives, from volunteering to doing charity work, as well as Inclusion & Diversity networks
  • International and cross-border expert teams guiding you in client engagements


    • Interested? Make your move – apply online and attach your CV and letter of application in English. We look forward to receiving your application.

You’ll be part of or lead project teams that drive impactful solutions, support regulatory compliance, and foster client success.Your specific role and responsibilities will be tailored to each engagement, allowing you to bring your expertise where it’s needed most and develop your skills in a challenging and rewarding environment.Responsibilities:Drive technical transformation to align with our clients' business goals and compliance processes.Contribute to solution design, system integration, and implementation of leading vendor platforms and in-house solutions.Serve as a functional and technical expert in financial crime prevention for client engagements.Apply strong problem-solving, analytical, and functional expertise to address complex challenges in financial crime preventionProfessional Requirements:Master or Bachelor’s degree from a technical or business schoolYou would have a minimum of 3-5 years of experience within technology implementations and advisory in financial crime prevention including AML, transaction monitoring, sanctions, KYC and fraud.Hands-on experience with one or more leading financial crime technology solutions (e.g., NICE Actimize, Oracle FCCM, SAS, FICO TONBELLER, Pega, Quantexa, or similar).Understanding of data management, ETL, and analytics tools, experience with SQL and Python is a plus.Strong analytical and problem-solving skills High drive, entrepreneurial attitude and good interpersonal skills Strong verbal and written communication skills in English; proficiency in Swedish is highly advantageousResident in SwedenWhat is in it for you:We believe in bringing the best to our clients through continuous learning & improvement – from basic skills to industry-specific contentMentoring and continuous evaluation that ensure your career continues to progressWe offer a performance management process that focuses on your strengths, progress and career possibilitiesFlexible work arrangements for maintaining work-life balanceExtensive wellbeing program, that boosts your overall wellbeing, energy and ability to work in a sustainable wayOpportunities to get involved in corporate citizenship initiatives, from volunteering to doing charity work, as well as Inclusion & Diversity networksInternational and cross-border expert teams guiding you in client engagementsInterested? Make your move – apply online and attach your CV and letter of application in English. We look forward to receiving your application.

companyDescription:
emagine is a high-end business & IT consulting company challenging the way businesses leverage high-end expertise.

Enabled by our vast network of expert consultants, we tailor our services to the unique needs of each business, powering progress, solving challenges, and delivering real results.

As the world evolves, scalability becomes increasingly important in modern, technology-driven organizations. And it is around this critical need that emagine stands out with a unique business model and delivery capacity.

With emagine, companies gain the flexibility needed to navigate and succeed in a complex and ever-changing technological landscape. Through a seamless and tailored delivery model, we help you scale your business.

We help organizations across all industries and sectors; these are our services:
• Team extension: Scale with dedicated consultants on time & material
• Nearshoring as a Service: Leverage a fully scalable development department in Poland 
• Teams: Power up with a dedicated team to help you build, develop and execute your projects
• Managed Services: Let us tailor and manage your projects, delivering on your specific requirements and needs

emagine was founded in 1989 and has a long track record of delivering expertise and business impact for blue-chip companies across Europe.

Today, we are 900+ permanent employees working from departments in 10 countries. Furthermore, we own three state-of-the-art nearshore centres in Poland and one offshore centre in India.

With 40.000 experts in our network and 4500+ partnered consultants on active contracts, we currently help our 500+ clients worldwide with high-end expertise.
descriptionHtml:
Hi,

emagine is looking for a Business Architect to join our client’s cross-functional Digital Procurement team.

You’ll play a key role in translating business needs into scalable technical solutions in close collaboration with developers and business stakeholders across the Source-to-Pay domain. This is a hands-on, high-impact role where you’ll work closely with business functions to map and understand procurement, inventory, and logistics processes – and help shape how they are enabled through our digital platforms and third party systems. You’ll act as a bridge between strategic process understanding and digital development through solution architecture work and planning activities together with the development team.

Required technical skills:

  • Experience with Spring Boot and REST API development
  • Experience building event-driven systems and working with microservices
  • Technocally grounded - ideally with a background in software development/solution architecture

Required domain experience (minimum one of the following):

  • Hands-on experience with Procure-to-Pay (P2P) process implementations (focus: Purchasing, Inventory, Warehouse)
  • ERP system projects in Process industry companies
  • Experience from SAP S/4HANA implementation projects

Profile & skills:

  • Strong communication and process mapping skills – you’re comfortable speaking with business stakeholders and translating their needs
  • Technically grounded – ideally with a background in software development
  • Used to working closely with agile development teams
  • A team player – humble, curious, and easy to collaborate with

If this sounds interesting: get back to me with your CV, a short motivation, availability and your price.

companyDescription: Cavotec is a leading cleantech company that designs and manufactures automation and electrification solutions to enable the decarbonisation of ports and industrial applications. Backed by 50 years of experience, our systems ensure safe, efficient and sustainable operations for a wide variety of customers and applications worldwide. Learn more at cavotec.com.
companySlogan: We connect the future.
descriptionHtml:
Agigo is recruiting a Finance Manager Norway to Cavotec Group, a leading cleantech company that designs and delivers connection and electrification solutions to enable the decarbonization of ports and industrial applications. Backed by more than 40 years of experience, their systems ensure safe, efficient and sustainable operations for a wide variety of customers and applications worldwide.


Cavotec is listed on the Nasdaq OMX Stockholm with Bure Equity as one of the major shareholders. The headquarters is in Stockholm with locations in more than 30 countries around the world.


This position is based at their office at Fagerstagatan 5, in Spånga (Stockholm area).


The role

We are looking for a driven and hands-on finance professional to execute the senior accounting, closing, reporting and invoicing functions for our Norwegian entity. You will oversee accounts receivable, accounts payable, cash flow management and forecasting, while also supporting business analysis, sustainability reporting and compliance tasks.


Working closely with colleagues in Stockholm and our head office in Lugano, you will play a key role in securing high-quality financial reporting and smooth operations in a dynamic, international matrix environment.


Responsibilities

  • Ensuring timely and correct day-to-day accounting, customer invoicing, accounts receivables collections, accounts payables processing and cash planning
  • Preparation of monthly, quarterly, yearly financial statements according to internal policies and deadlines, together with the corresponding internal and external reporting’s
  • Ensuring all external statutory financial requirements are being met, such as annual close and audit (PWC), necessary tax filings (i.e. VAT, CIT) and applicable statutory reporting
  • Preparation of reports & analysis for the business
  • Monitor and partially operationally execute sustainability reporting, as well as new customer/supplier KYC and compliance tasks
  • Maintain relationships with local banks, tax advisors, auditors, insurance brokers, legal advisors, payroll providers and other external and internal stakeholders
  • Support the implementation or exchange of external providers and IT tools, as applicable
  • Cooperation with key team members in head office in Lugano and Stockholm city


Qualifications

  • Degree in Business Administration, or equivalent
  • 5+ years working experience in a similar role
  • Familiar with IFRS & Norwegian Reporting requirements
  • Excellent English and Norwegian language skills is mandatory
  • Familiarity with Microsoft NAV or large ERPs
  • Excellent Excel skills are essential


You are

We are looking for a highly skilled and motivated professional with strong experience in ERP systems (Navision preferred) and excellent Excel capabilities. You are IT-savvy, detail-oriented and thrive in a role that requires both autonomy and collaboration. With strong problem-solving abilities, goal orientation and proactive mindset, you manage your time effectively while ensuring accuracy in everything you do. Excellent communication skills and a true team spirit will make you a valuable contributor to our fast-paced and international environment


About Cavotec

Cavotec wants to contribute to a future world that is cleaner, safer and more efficient by providing innovative connection and electrification solutions to enable the decarbonization of ports and industrial applications.


We thrive by shaping future expectations in the areas we are active in. Our credibility comes from our application expertise, dedication to innovation and world class operations. Our success rests on the core values we live by, Integrity, Accountability, Performance and Team Work.


Cavotec’s personnel, located in some 30 countries around the world, represents a large number of cultures, and provides customers with local support, backed by the Group’s global network of engineering expertise. Read more here.


About Agigo

Agigo is a consulting and recruitment agency specializing in finance and accounting in the Stockholm area. Through a modern and proven approach, we create a simple, smooth, and transparent process. Agigo's vision is straightforward; we aim to be a reliable and seamless career partner for our candidates and ensure through proactive efforts that you, as a client, can always trust that we prioritize your needs.


Application

Does this sound intriguing? Apply now. If you have any questions, feel free to contact the responsible recruiter, Rebecca Benholm, at rebecca.benholm@agigo.se, or 076-000 63 80.

companyDescription:
Trustly was launched in 2008 and has grown rapidly over the past 16 years to become a global leader in Open Banking Payment solutions. With a mission to make online payments as seamless as possible, Trustly offers an innovative payment platform, bridging the gap between consumers and merchants. Its technology ensures that transactions are processed in real-time, providing both speed and security for all parties involved.

Trustly's dedication to revolutionizing the payments industry is reflected in its collaborations with major brands such as PayPal, eBay, and Hargreaves Lansdown in Europe and FanDuel, T-Mobile, and Coinbase in North America. To date, Trustly has transformed the performance and experience of payments for over 9,000 merchants in 30+ markets, connecting them to 650+ million consumers through 12,000 banks.

Trustly is a licensed Payment Institution under the second payment services directive (PSD2). It operates under the supervision of the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority in the EU, EEA and the Financial Conduct Authority in the UK. In the US, Trustly is state-regulated as required to serve its target markets.
companySlogan: Fast, simple and secure online banking payments
descriptionHtml:
Trustly is leading the human-centric payments revolution. To us, this means passionately building the most convenient, intelligent and responsible way of paying for things online. Whether it’s for shopping, paying subscriptions, funding trading accounts, booking airfare, playing online games and much more – we’re all about a better way to pay. At our core, we are a tech company with industry-leading tech capabilities. But, it’s the ingenuity of our people that makes us leaders in our field. Thus, our appetite for innovation will never be anything less than fierce. Trustly is steadily growing as it connects thousands of businesses with hundreds of millions of people. And with a strong presence across Europe and the Americas, we are leading the human-centric payment revolution as a truly global team.


About GtM Tooling Team

At Trustly, the GtM Tooling team managing Salesforce sits within the Global Revenue Operations function, but plays a crucial role in supporting our broader operations teams - including Merchant Services, Customer Support, Underwriting, and KYC. We’re a small but mighty in-house team of experts, passionate about driving efficiency, scalability, and outstanding customer experiences.


About the role

We’re looking for a skilled and proactive Salesforce Automations Engineer to help Trustly’s operational engine run smoothly. In this role, you’ll be the backbone of our Salesforce environment, building and maintaining the solutions that empower teams to onboard merchants quickly, manage cases efficiently, and uphold the highest compliance standards.


You’ll partner closely with stakeholders across Operations, Underwriting, Commercial, and RevOps to gather requirements, translate them into robust Salesforce solutions, and ensure our platform is continuously evolving to meet the needs of a growing, global business. This is an opportunity to make a significant impact in a dynamic environment, driving improvements that directly affect merchant experience and internal productivity.


What you’ll do
  • Design, develop, and maintain Salesforce solutions tailored to Trustly’s operational workflows (e.g., case management, merchant onboarding, KYC)
  • Configure Salesforce features such as flows, process builders, validation rules, and custom objects to optimize efficiency
  • Gather and translate business requirements from Operations and RevOps stakeholders into clear technical specifications
  • Support integrations with third-party systems critical to Trustly’s merchant and customer lifecycle
  • Develop and maintain APEX code, Visualforce components, and Lightning pages as needed
  • Troubleshoot issues, perform debugging, and manage data migrations or cleanup projects
  • Drive adoption of Salesforce best practices and provide hands-on training and documentation to end-users
  • Monitor platform health, proactively identify improvement opportunities, and ensure high-quality standards across all solutions


Who you are
  • You have 3+ years of experience as a Salesforce Developer in a fast-paced environment
  • You’re comfortable working end-to-end, from gathering requirements to building, testing, and deploying solutions
  • Skilled in declarative Salesforce configuration, and proficient in Apex, Visualforce, and Lightning development
  • You enjoy collaborating with a variety of stakeholders and can translate complex business needs into scalable technical solutions
  • You’re detail-oriented, highly accountable, and thrive in a small, high-impact team
  • Salesforce certifications (Administrator, Platform App Builder, or Platform) 
  • Proficiency in web technologies including JavaScript, HTML, CSS, etc., is a plus
  • Developer I/II are a plus as well as previous experience working within payments or with operational processes such as customer service, KYC, and onboarding workflows
  • Proficiency in English



Our Fantastic Benefits

🌴 20 to 30 days of holiday (depending on location) to support a healthy work-life balance

🥳 Monthly team outing allowance to enjoy social events with your colleagues

👶 Parental leave top-up additional support for new parents

🥐 Daily breakfast and on-site perks to make your workday smoother

💚 Well being support our health allowance covers gym memberships, massages, and much more to help you feel your best

PLUS additional benefits designed to enhance your work-life experience!


Shape Your Role and Make an Impact

Join a dynamic environment where you can take ownership, drive change, and continuously embrace new challenges. We’re looking for dedicated and highly motivated individuals who thrive in a fast-paced environment and enjoy collaborating across different areas of the organisation.

If your skills and experience align with this role, we’d love to hear from you! Apply now and submit your CV in English.
